Use when the user runs /design-sync or says \"sync my design system to Claude Design\".","argumentHint":"[<project hint, e.g. \"Acme DS\">]"},{"name":"dataviz","description":"Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: \"chart\", \"graph\", \"plot\", \"data viz\", \"visualization\", \"dashboard\", \"analytics\", \"visualize data\", \"categorical colors\", \"sequential / diverging palette\", \"stat tile\", \"sparkline\", \"heatmap\", \"legend\", \"axis\", \"tooltip\", \"chart colors\", \"color by series\".","argumentHint":""},{"name":"update-config","description":"Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\"from now on when X\", \"each time X\", \"whenever X\", \"before/after X\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\"allow X\", \"add permission\", \"move permission to\"), env vars (\"set X=Y\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \"allow npm commands\", \"add bq permission to global settings\", \"move permission to user settings\", \"set DEBUG=true\", \"when claude stops show X\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.","argumentHint":""},{"name":"verify","description":"Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.","argumentHint":""},{"name":"debug","description":"Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues","argumentHint":"[issue description]"},{"name":"code-review","description":"Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.","argumentHint":"[low|medium|high|xhigh|max|ultra] [--fix] [--comment] [<target>]"},{"name":"simplify","description":"Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_011QN1P5VHVr53zCJ8Yyudkf","type":"tool_result","content":"1	# Deep Start Review Loop Presentation Contract
2	
3	Presentation source of truth for `/deep:review`. The command router owns only mode selection, safety gates, and asset loading. Workflow YAML owns execution. This file owns visible prompts, dashboard/checkpoint layout, result displays, examples, and next-step suggestions.
4	
5	## 1. Startup Presentation
6	
7	This workflow gathers all setup inputs in one prompt. Confirm mode still includes multiple approval gates after setup; only the setup round-trip is consolidated here.
8	
9	For `:confirm` or no suffix, the consolidated setup prompt MUST be the first visible response. No implementation or file-modifying tool calls before asking. Lightweight read-only discovery to suggest a spec folder or load prior context is allowed, then ask ALL questions immediately and wait.
10	
11	For `:auto`, do not emit the consolidated prompt by default. Resolve setup with the three-tier branch below, then load the auto YAML only after all required values are bound.
12	
13	### `:auto` Setup Resolution
14	
15	Setup contract: see `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/workflows/auto_mode_contract.md`.
16	
17	Under `execution_mode = AUTONOMOUS` (from the `:auto` suffix), follow the three-tier flow:
18	
19	1. **Tier 1 — Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, resolve `{artifact_dir}` and persist to `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-config.json` (shape: `reviewTarget`, `reviewTargetType`, `reviewDimensions`, `specFolder`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`, `stopPolicy`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `lineageMode`, `resource_map.emit`, `config.executor.*`), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_review_auto.yaml`. End §0.
20	
21	2. **Tier 2 — Targeted ask** (contract §1): when 1-2 required fields are genuinely ambiguous AND no default exists, emit ONE narrow question per ambiguous field. Command-specific Tier-2-eligible fields (per the Default Resolution Table below): `review_target_type`, `spec_folder`. **Ordering rule**: if `review_target_type` is ambiguous, ask only for `review_target_type` first — the answer may make `spec_folder` self-evident on the next Tier 1 pass. Missing `review_target` is absence, not ambiguity — go to Tier 3.
22	
23	3. **Tier 3 — Fail fast** (contract §4): emit the named-missing-inputs error format with `/deep:review:auto` as the command name. Exit non-zero. Do not load YAML.
24	
25	`:confirm` path stays unchanged — see the Consolidated Setup Prompt section below.
26	
27	### PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS Schema (for `:auto` non-interactive dispatch)
28	
29	The dispatched prompt body may contain one structured marker block. Parse it before applying defaults.
30	
31	```yaml
32	PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:
33	  review_target: <spec-folder>
34	  review_target_type: spec-folder  # one of: spec-folder | skill | agent | track | files
35	  review_dimensions: all  # or comma-separated subset: correctness, security, traceability, maintainability
36	  spec_folder: existing  # one of: existing | new | update-related | phase-folder, or an explicit specs/.opencode/specs path
37	  execution_mode: AUTONOMOUS  # from :auto suffix
38	 lineage_mode: auto  # one of: auto | resume | restart
39	  maxIterations: 10
40	  convergenceThreshold: 0.10
41	  stop_policy: convergence  # one of: convergence | max-iterations
42	  executor: native  # one of: native | cli-opencode | cli-claude-code | cli-opencode
43	  executor_model: ""  # optional, executor-specific (cli-opencode e.g. xiaomi-token-plan-ams/mimo-v2.5-pro, minimax-coding-plan/MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed)
44	  executor_config_dir: ""  # optional, cli-claude-code only; maps to CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR
45	  executor_reasoning: ""  # optional
46	  executor_service_tier: ""  # optional
47	  executor_timeout: 900  # optional
48	  resource_map_emit: true  # optional
49	```
50	
51	Rules:
52	
53	- Any unspecified field falls back to its documented default.
54	- Marker fields take precedence over `$ARGUMENTS` flags because the caller explicitly bound setup in the prompt body.
55	- Unknown fields are warnings, not errors.
56	- Malformed lines, including missing `:`, emit a parse error naming the offending line. Known fields parsed before the error may still be used, and unresolved fields continue to Tier 2 or Tier 3.
57	- Empty strings count as unresolved for required fields.
58	- Compact legacy answer strings are only for the consolidated `:confirm` prompt. They are not a `:auto` marker format.
59	
60	### Default Resolution Table
61	
62	| Field | Required | Resolves Via | Default | Tier-2 Candidate |
63	|-------|----------|--------------|---------|------------------|
64	| `review_target` | Y | `$ARGUMENTS` first positional target, or marker `review_target` | none | N |
65	| `review_target_type` | Y | marker `review_target_type`, or auto-detect from `review_target` | inferred only | Y, when target is present but ambiguous |
66	| `review_dimensions` | Y | flag `--dims` if supported by caller, marker `review_dimensions`, or default | `"all"` | N |
67	| `spec_folder` | Y | flag `--spec-folder`, marker `spec_folder`, `scope-extract` → a target/scope path that resolves to an existing spec folder (auto-bound, per `auto_mode_contract` §1 source 3), or target path when target is a spec folder | none | Y, when target is not a spec folder |
68	| `execution_mode` | Y | attached suffix `:auto` or marker `execution_mode` | `AUTONOMOUS` under `:auto` | N |
69	| `lineage_mode` | Y | flag `--restart`, flag `--lineage-mode=auto|resume|restart`, marker `lineage_mode`, or default | `auto` | N |
70	| `maxIterations` | Y | flag `--max-iterations`, marker `maxIterations`, or default | `7` | N |
71	| `convergenceThreshold` | Y | flag `--convergence`, marker `convergenceThreshold`, or default | `0.10` | N |
72	| `stop_policy` | Y | flag `--stop-policy=convergence|max-iterations`, marker `stop_policy`, or default | `convergence` | N |
73	| `executor` | N | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, config file, or default | `native` | N |
74	| `executor_model` | N | flag `--model`, marker `executor_model`, or executor-specific validation | none | N |
75	| `executor_config_dir` | N | flag `--config-dir`, marker `executor_config_dir`, or executor-specific default | none | N |
76	| `executor_reasoning` | N | flag `--reasoning-effort`, marker `executor_reasoning`, or executor default | none | N |
77	| `executor_service_tier` | N | flag `--service-tier`, marker `executor_service_tier`, or executor default | none | N |
78	| `executor_timeout` | N | flag `--executor-timeout`, marker `executor_timeout`, or default | `900` | N |
79	| `resource_map_emit` | N | flag `--no-resource-map`, marker `resource_map_emit`, or default | `true` | N |
80	| `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` | N | internal flag `--fanout-lineage-artifact-dir=PATH` or marker `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` | none | N |
81	| `fanout_executors` | N | repeatable `--executor=<type>` flags or `--executors=<json>`; each group accepts `--model`, `--config-dir`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`, `--iters`, `--label`, `--count` | none (single-executor when absent) | N |
82	| `fanout_concurrency` | N | flag `--concurrency=N` | `2` | N |
83	
84	**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Review fan-out uses strongest-restriction: any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL. Native and CLI fan-out lineages run under `fanout-run.cjs` in the capped pool so every lineage shares retries, artifact checks, and stop-policy validation. The native adapter calls the `deep/review` command with the internal `--fanout-lineage-artifact-dir` override; it must not dispatch the LEAF `@deep-review` agent or a pasted phase-running prompt as a full-loop sub-agent. When fan-out includes `cli-opencode`, that lineage is an explicit parallel-detached OpenCode session with its own lineage directory and session id; this is the documented exception to the cli-opencode self-invocation guard. Single-executor `cli-opencode` remains cross-runtime only.
85	
86	**Lifecycle policy:** `lineage_mode=auto` preserves the existing classifier behavior, `lineage_mode=resume` continues a valid packet, and `lineage_mode=restart` archives the resolved `{artifact_dir}` before any config write, phase init, or fan-out spawn. In `:auto`, an explicit `--restart` or `--lineage-mode=restart` flag is operator authorization for this archive move, so do not ask for a second confirmation. Preserve rollback by moving the timestamped archive back to `review/` if needed. A restart request must never silently downgrade to resume; if the archive step cannot run, halt with `STATUS=FAIL`.
87	
88	**Stop policy:** `stop_policy=convergence` preserves normal deep-review behavior: legal convergence may stop before `maxIterations`. `stop_policy=max-iterations` makes convergence signals telemetry-only until the iteration ceiling; before `maxIterations`, broaden the next angle instead of synthesizing. Terminal manual pause, user stop, unrecoverable error, and `maxIterationsReached` still stop.

233	## 2. Dashboard / Checkpoint Layout
234	
235	### Deep Review
236	
237	Conduct autonomous iterative code review with convergence detection. Each iteration dispatches a fresh LEAF agent (`@deep-review`) that reads externalized state, performs focused review work across configured dimensions, and writes findings to files.
238	
239	### Convergence Threshold Semantics
240	
241	**Default:** 0.10 (weighted P0/P1/P2 severity ratio)
242	
243	**Semantic:** `convergenceThreshold` compares new severity-weighted findings (P0=10, P1=5, P2=1) against accumulated findings. Lower = more iterations / higher signal threshold.
244	
245	**NOT INTERCHANGEABLE with siblings:**
246	- `deep-research` uses 0.05 default on newInfoRatio (negative-knowledge emphasis)
247	- `deep-ai-council` (proposed) uses 0.20 default on adjudicator-verdict stability
248	
249	Carrying threshold expectations across siblings will cause unexpected iteration counts. See 116 research at `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/z_archive/116-deep-skill-evolution/006-deep-stack-cross-cutting/001-unique-value-differentiation/research/research.md` §2 F56/F78, §5 Recommendation, and §6 Parity Invariants.
250	
251	```yaml
252	role: Deep Review Loop Manager
253	purpose: Run iterative review cycles until convergence or max iterations
254	action: Execute YAML workflow managing init, loop, synthesis, and save phases
255	operating_mode:
256	  workflow: iterative_loop
257	  compliance: MANDATORY
258	  execution: autonomous_or_interactive
259	  validation: convergence_detection
260	```
261	
262	### Purpose
263	
264	Run an iterative loop for code review: Initialize the review packet under `{artifact_dir}` (resolved via `resolveArtifactRoot()` — root specs use `{spec_folder}/review/`; child phases and sub-phases use the flat `{spec_folder}/review/` directory on the first matching run and `{spec_folder}/review/{packet}-pt-{NN}/` only when prior non-matching content requires a separate packet), dispatch `@deep-review` agent per iteration, evaluate convergence across review dimensions, synthesize findings into `{artifact_dir}/review-report.md`, and emit `{artifact_dir}/resource-map.md` at convergence unless `--no-resource-map` disables it. Use when auditing code, specs, skills, agents, or tracks for quality and release readiness.
265	
266	### Contract
267	
268	**Inputs:** `$ARGUMENTS` -- Review target with optional flags and mode suffix
269	**Outputs:** Spec folder with `{artifact_dir}/` packet (flat-first `{spec_folder}/review/`, with `{spec_folder}/review/{packet}-pt-{NN}/` only for separated nested-phase packets), `review-report.md`, optional `resource-map.md`, state files, and `STATUS=<OK|FAIL|CANCELLED>`
270	
271	### Workflow Overview
272	
273	| Phase | Name | Purpose | Outputs |
274	|-------|------|---------|---------|
275	| Init | Initialize | Scope discovery, resolve files, create config + strategy with review dimensions | Review packet in `{spec_folder}/review/` |
276	| Loop | Iterate | Dispatch @deep-review agent per dimension, evaluate review convergence + quality guards | `review/iterations/iteration-NNN.md` files, `review/deep-review-dashboard.md` |
277	| Synth | Synthesize | Build finding registry, emit `review/resource-map.md`, deduplicate, compile `review/review-report.md` | `review/resource-map.md`, `review/review-report.md` (9 sections) |
278	| Save | Preserve | Refresh continuity update in canonical spec docs | canonical spec doc updated via `generate-context.js` |
279	
280	### Execution Modes
281	
282	| Mode | Invocation | Behavior |
283	|------|-----------|----------|
284	| `:auto` | `/deep:review:auto "target"` | All iterations without approval |
285	| `:confirm` | `/deep:review:confirm "target"` | Multi-gate review at setup, iteration, and synthesis |
286	| (default) | `/deep:review "target"` | Ask user to choose mode during setup |
287	
288	### Key Behaviors
289	
290	#### Autonomous Mode (`:auto`)
291	
292	- Executes all iterations without user approval gates
293	- Self-validates at each convergence checkpoint
294	- Makes informed decisions on dimension ordering and iteration focus
295	- Documents all significant decisions in `review/deep-review-dashboard.md`
296	- Stops when convergence threshold is met or max iterations reached
297	
298	#### Interactive Mode (`:confirm`)
299

372	### Key Features
373	
374	#### Review Dimensions
375	- Iterative (multi-pass) code/spec audit across configurable dimensions
376	- Dimension ordering follows risk priority (correctness and security first)
377	- Each dimension can be individually included or excluded
378	
379	#### Severity and Verdicts
380	- Three severity levels: P0 (Blocker), P1 (Required), P2 (Suggestion)
381	- Three verdicts: PASS, CONDITIONAL, FAIL
382	- PASS may include `hasAdvisories=true` when P2 findings exist
383	- P0 override: new P0 findings block convergence regardless of threshold
384	
385	#### Convergence Detection
386	- Severity-weighted `newFindingsRatio` with rolling average
387	- MAD noise-floor test for robust churn detection
388	- Dimension coverage as convergence signal (all required dimensions must be reviewed)
389	- Three binary quality gates (evidence, scope, coverage) must pass before STOP
390	
391	#### Adversarial Self-Check
392	- Runs on all P0 findings to validate evidence quality
393	- Cross-reference verification across spec/code/test boundaries
394	- Finding deduplication and progressive synthesis
395	
396	#### Agent Model
397	- Dispatches `@deep-review` LEAF agent per iteration (fresh context each time)
398	- Externalized state via JSONL + strategy files (no context degradation)
399	- Review target is READ-ONLY (agent never modifies reviewed code)
400	- Does NOT proceed to implementation (outputs remediation plan for `/speckit:plan`)
401

404	Convergence thresholds and recovery settings used by this command are a governed maintenance surface managed by the offline loop optimizer (042.004). The optimizer tunes deterministic numeric thresholds offline against real run traces and emits advisory-only candidate patches.

406	**Key constraints:**
407	- Optimization is offline only -- it does not run during live review sessions
408	- Promotion is advisory-only until replay fixtures and behavioral suites exist
409	- Prompt optimization is deferred future work (Phase 4b) and will use generated prompt packs, never direct agent markdown mutation

414	- Convergence reference: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md`

Presentation source of truth for `/deep:review`. The command router owns only mode selection, safety gates, and asset loading. Workflow YAML owns execution. This file owns visible prompts, dashboard/checkpoint layout, result displays, examples, and next-step suggestions.

## 1. Startup Presentation

This workflow gathers all setup inputs in one prompt. Confirm mode still includes multiple approval gates after setup; only the setup round-trip is consolidated here.

For `:confirm` or no suffix, the consolidated setup prompt MUST be the first visible response. No implementation or file-modifying tool calls before asking. Lightweight read-only discovery to suggest a spec folder or load prior context is allowed, then ask ALL questions immediately and wait.

For `:auto`, do not emit the consolidated prompt by default. Resolve setup with the three-tier branch below, then load the auto YAML only after all required values are bound.

### `:auto` Setup Resolution

Setup contract: see `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/workflows/auto_mode_contract.md`.

Under `execution_mode = AUTONOMOUS` (from the `:auto` suffix), follow the three-tier flow:

1. **Tier 1 — Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, resolve `{artifact_dir}` and persist to `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-config.json` (shape: `reviewTarget`, `reviewTargetType`, `reviewDimensions`, `specFolder`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`, `stopPolicy`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `lineageMode`, `resource_map.emit`, `config.executor.*`), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_review_auto.yaml`. End §0.

2. **Tier 2 — Targeted ask** (contract §1): when 1-2 required fields are genuinely ambiguous AND no default exists, emit ONE narrow question per ambiguous field. Command-specific Tier-2-eligible fields (per the Default Resolution Table below): `review_target_type`, `spec_folder`. **Ordering rule**: if `review_target_type` is ambiguous, ask only for `review_target_type` first — the answer may make `spec_folder` self-evident on the next Tier 1 pass. Missing `review_target` is absence, not ambiguity — go to Tier 3.

3. **Tier 3 — Fail fast** (contract §4): emit the named-missing-inputs error format with `/deep:review:auto` as the command name. Exit non-zero. Do not load YAML.

`:confirm` path stays unchanged — see the Consolidated Setup Prompt section below.

### PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS Schema (for `:auto` non-interactive dispatch)

The dispatched prompt body may contain one structured marker block. Parse it before applying defaults.

```yaml
PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:
  review_target: <spec-folder>
  review_target_type: spec-folder  # one of: spec-folder | skill | agent | track | files
  review_dimensions: all  # or comma-separated subset: correctness, security, traceability, maintainability
  spec_folder: existing  # one of: existing | new | update-related | phase-folder, or an explicit specs/.opencode/specs path
  execution_mode: AUTONOMOUS  # from :auto suffix
 lineage_mode: auto  # one of: auto | resume | restart
  maxIterations: 10
  convergenceThreshold: 0.10
  stop_policy: convergence  # one of: convergence | max-iterations
  executor: native  # one of: native | cli-opencode | cli-claude-code | cli-opencode
  executor_model: ""  # optional, executor-specific (cli-opencode e.g. xiaomi-token-plan-ams/mimo-v2.5-pro, minimax-coding-plan/MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed)
  executor_config_dir: ""  # optional, cli-claude-code only; maps to CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR
  executor_reasoning: ""  # optional
  executor_service_tier: ""  # optional
  executor_timeout: 900  # optional
  resource_map_emit: true  # optional
```

Rules:

- Any unspecified field falls back to its documented default.
- Marker fields take precedence over `$ARGUMENTS` flags because the caller explicitly bound setup in the prompt body.
- Unknown fields are warnings, not errors.
- Malformed lines, including missing `:`, emit a parse error naming the offending line. Known fields parsed before the error may still be used, and unresolved fields continue to Tier 2 or Tier 3.
- Empty strings count as unresolved for required fields.
- Compact legacy answer strings are only for the consolidated `:confirm` prompt. They are not a `:auto` marker format.

### Default Resolution Table

| Field | Required | Resolves Via | Default | Tier-2 Candidate |
|-------|----------|--------------|---------|------------------|
| `review_target` | Y | `$ARGUMENTS` first positional target, or marker `review_target` | none | N |
| `review_target_type` | Y | marker `review_target_type`, or auto-detect from `review_target` | inferred only | Y, when target is present but ambiguous |
| `review_dimensions` | Y | flag `--dims` if supported by caller, marker `review_dimensions`, or default | `"all"` | N |
| `spec_folder` | Y | flag `--spec-folder`, marker `spec_folder`, `scope-extract` → a target/scope path that resolves to an existing spec folder (auto-bound, per `auto_mode_contract` §1 source 3), or target path when target is a spec folder | none | Y, when target is not a spec folder |
| `execution_mode` | Y | attached suffix `:auto` or marker `execution_mode` | `AUTONOMOUS` under `:auto` | N |
| `lineage_mode` | Y | flag `--restart`, flag `--lineage-mode=auto|resume|restart`, marker `lineage_mode`, or default | `auto` | N |
| `maxIterations` | Y | flag `--max-iterations`, marker `maxIterations`, or default | `7` | N |
| `convergenceThreshold` | Y | flag `--convergence`, marker `convergenceThreshold`, or default | `0.10` | N |
| `stop_policy` | Y | flag `--stop-policy=convergence|max-iterations`, marker `stop_policy`, or default | `convergence` | N |
| `executor` | N | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, config file, or default | `native` | N |
| `executor_model` | N | flag `--model`, marker `executor_model`, or executor-specific validation | none | N |
| `executor_config_dir` | N | flag `--config-dir`, marker `executor_config_dir`, or executor-specific default | none | N |
| `executor_reasoning` | N | flag `--reasoning-effort`, marker `executor_reasoning`, or executor default | none | N |
| `executor_service_tier` | N | flag `--service-tier`, marker `executor_service_tier`, or executor default | none | N |
| `executor_timeout` | N | flag `--executor-timeout`, marker `executor_timeout`, or default | `900` | N |
| `resource_map_emit` | N | flag `--no-resource-map`, marker `resource_map_emit`, or default | `true` | N |
| `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` | N | internal flag `--fanout-lineage-artifact-dir=PATH` or marker `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` | none | N |
| `fanout_executors` | N | repeatable `--executor=<type>` flags or `--executors=<json>`; each group accepts `--model`, `--config-dir`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`, `--iters`, `--label`, `--count` | none (single-executor when absent) | N |
| `fanout_concurrency` | N | flag `--concurrency=N` | `2` | N |

**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Review fan-out uses strongest-restriction: any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL. Native and CLI fan-out lineages run under `fanout-run.cjs` in the capped pool so every lineage shares retries, artifact checks, and stop-policy validation. The native adapter calls the `deep/review` command with the internal `--fanout-lineage-artifact-dir` override; it must not dispatch the LEAF `@deep-review` agent or a pasted phase-running prompt as a full-loop sub-agent. When fan-out includes `cli-opencode`, that lineage is an explicit parallel-detached OpenCode session with its own lineage directory and session id; this is the documented exception to the cli-opencode self-invocation guard. Single-executor `cli-opencode` remains cross-runtime only.

**Lifecycle policy:** `lineage_mode=auto` preserves the existing classifier behavior, `lineage_mode=resume` continues a valid packet, and `lineage_mode=restart` archives the resolved `{artifact_dir}` before any config write, phase init, or fan-out spawn. In `:auto`, an explicit `--restart` or `--lineage-mode=restart` flag is operator authorization for this archive move, so do not ask for a second confirmation. Preserve rollback by moving the timestamped archive back to `review/` if needed. A restart request must never silently downgrade to resume; if the archive step cannot run, halt with `STATUS=FAIL`.

**Stop policy:** `stop_policy=convergence` preserves normal deep-review behavior: legal convergence may stop before `maxIterations`. `stop_policy=max-iterations` makes convergence signals telemetry-only until the iteration ceiling; before `maxIterations`, broaden the next angle instead of synthesizing. Terminal manual pause, user stop, unrecoverable error, and `maxIterationsReached` still stop.

## 2. Dashboard / Checkpoint Layout

### Deep Review

Conduct autonomous iterative code review with convergence detection. Each iteration dispatches a fresh LEAF agent (`@deep-review`) that reads externalized state, performs focused review work across configured dimensions, and writes findings to files.

### Convergence Threshold Semantics

**Default:** 0.10 (weighted P0/P1/P2 severity ratio)

**Semantic:** `convergenceThreshold` compares new severity-weighted findings (P0=10, P1=5, P2=1) against accumulated findings. Lower = more iterations / higher signal threshold.

**NOT INTERCHANGEABLE with siblings:**
- `deep-research` uses 0.05 default on newInfoRatio (negative-knowledge emphasis)
- `deep-ai-council` (proposed) uses 0.20 default on adjudicator-verdict stability

Carrying threshold expectations across siblings will cause unexpected iteration counts. See 116 research at `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/z_archive/116-deep-skill-evolution/006-deep-stack-cross-cutting/001-unique-value-differentiation/research/research.md` §2 F56/F78, §5 Recommendation, and §6 Parity Invariants.

```yaml
role: Deep Review Loop Manager
purpose: Run iterative review cycles until convergence or max iterations
action: Execute YAML workflow managing init, loop, synthesis, and save phases
operating_mode:
  workflow: iterative_loop
  compliance: MANDATORY
  execution: autonomous_or_interactive
  validation: convergence_detection
```

### Purpose

Run an iterative loop for code review: Initialize the review packet under `{artifact_dir}` (resolved via `resolveArtifactRoot()` — root specs use `{spec_folder}/review/`; child phases and sub-phases use the flat `{spec_folder}/review/` directory on the first matching run and `{spec_folder}/review/{packet}-pt-{NN}/` only when prior non-matching content requires a separate packet), dispatch `@deep-review` agent per iteration, evaluate convergence across review dimensions, synthesize findings into `{artifact_dir}/review-report.md`, and emit `{artifact_dir}/resource-map.md` at convergence unless `--no-resource-map` disables it. Use when auditing code, specs, skills, agents, or tracks for quality and release readiness.

### Contract

**Inputs:** `$ARGUMENTS` -- Review target with optional flags and mode suffix
**Outputs:** Spec folder with `{artifact_dir}/` packet (flat-first `{spec_folder}/review/`, with `{spec_folder}/review/{packet}-pt-{NN}/` only for separated nested-phase packets), `review-report.md`, optional `resource-map.md`, state files, and `STATUS=<OK|FAIL|CANCELLED>`

### Workflow Overview

| Phase | Name | Purpose | Outputs |
|-------|------|---------|---------|
| Init | Initialize | Scope discovery, resolve files, create config + strategy with review dimensions | Review packet in `{spec_folder}/review/` |
| Loop | Iterate | Dispatch @deep-review agent per dimension, evaluate review convergence + quality guards | `review/iterations/iteration-NNN.md` files, `review/deep-review-dashboard.md` |
| Synth | Synthesize | Build finding registry, emit `review/resource-map.md`, deduplicate, compile `review/review-report.md` | `review/resource-map.md`, `review/review-report.md` (9 sections) |
| Save | Preserve | Refresh continuity update in canonical spec docs | canonical spec doc updated via `generate-context.js` |

### Execution Modes

| Mode | Invocation | Behavior |
|------|-----------|----------|
| `:auto` | `/deep:review:auto "target"` | All iterations without approval |
| `:confirm` | `/deep:review:confirm "target"` | Multi-gate review at setup, iteration, and synthesis |
| (default) | `/deep:review "target"` | Ask user to choose mode during setup |

### Key Behaviors

#### Autonomous Mode (`:auto`)

- Executes all iterations without user approval gates
- Self-validates at each convergence checkpoint
- Makes informed decisions on dimension ordering and iteration focus
- Documents all significant decisions in `review/deep-review-dashboard.md`
- Stops when convergence threshold is met or max iterations reached

#### Interactive Mode (`:confirm`)

- Pauses after each iteration for user approval
- Presents iteration findings summary with dimension coverage
- Options at each gate: Approve, Review Details, Modify Scope, Skip Dimension, Abort
- Documents user decisions at each checkpoint
- Allows course correction of dimensions, depth, or scope throughout

### Memory Integration

#### Before Starting
- `memory_context({ input: review_target, intent: "understand" })` -- Load prior review context
- Inject results into strategy.md "Known Context" section

#### Code Context Bootstrap
- Use Code Graph (`code_graph_query`) to find relevant code examples before starting review
- Query: 2-5 word concept descriptions related to the review target
- Inject discovered code patterns into strategy.md "Known Context" section alongside memory findings

#### After Completing
- `node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js /tmp/save-context-data-<session-id>.json [spec-folder]`
- Verify that the canonical save routed continuity into the expected packet doc (decision-record.md / implementation-summary.md / handover.md)

#### Anchor Tags (Automatic)
`ANCHOR:deep-review-[target]`, `ANCHOR:findings`, `ANCHOR:convergence-report`

### Skill Reference

Full protocol documentation: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/SKILL.md`

Key references:
- Review contract: `deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml`
- Review strategy template: `deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_strategy.md`
- Review dashboard template: `deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_dashboard.md`
- Review config template: `deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_config.json`

## 3. Results Display

### Instructions

After setup phase passes, load and execute the appropriate YAML prompt based on execution_mode:

- **AUTONOMOUS**: `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_review_auto.yaml`
- **INTERACTIVE**: `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_review_confirm.yaml`

The YAML contains the full loop workflow: initialization, iteration dispatch, convergence detection, synthesis, and memory save.

### Review Success

```
Deep review complete.
Iterations: [N] | Stop reason: [converged|max_iterations|all_dimensions_clean]
Findings: P0=[N] P1=[N] P2=[N] | Verdict: [PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL] [PASS may include hasAdvisories=true]
Artifacts: review/review-report.md, review/resource-map.md (unless `--no-resource-map`), [N] iteration files in review/, continuity update in canonical spec docs refreshed
Ready for: /speckit:plan [remediation] (if FAIL/CONDITIONAL)
STATUS=OK PATH=[spec-folder-path]
```

### Failure

```
Error: [error description]  Phase: [phase name]
STATUS=FAIL ERROR="[message]"
```

### Error Handling

| Error | Action |
|-------|--------|
| Agent dispatch timeout | Retry once with reduced scope, then mark timeout |
| State file missing | Reconstruct from iteration files |
| 3+ consecutive failures | Halt loop, enter synthesis with partial findings |
| Memory save failure | Preserve the `review/` packet as backup |

### Key Features

#### Review Dimensions
- Iterative (multi-pass) code/spec audit across configurable dimensions
- Dimension ordering follows risk priority (correctness and security first)
- Each dimension can be individually included or excluded

#### Severity and Verdicts
- Three severity levels: P0 (Blocker), P1 (Required), P2 (Suggestion)
- Three verdicts: PASS, CONDITIONAL, FAIL
- PASS may include `hasAdvisories=true` when P2 findings exist
- P0 override: new P0 findings block convergence regardless of threshold

#### Convergence Detection
- Severity-weighted `newFindingsRatio` with rolling average
- MAD noise-floor test for robust churn detection
- Dimension coverage as convergence signal (all required dimensions must be reviewed)
- Three binary quality gates (evidence, scope, coverage) must pass before STOP

#### Adversarial Self-Check
- Runs on all P0 findings to validate evidence quality
- Cross-reference verification across spec/code/test boundaries
- Finding deduplication and progressive synthesis

#### Agent Model
- Dispatches `@deep-review` LEAF agent per iteration (fresh context each time)
- Externalized state via JSONL + strategy files (no context degradation)
- Review target is READ-ONLY (agent never modifies reviewed code)
- Does NOT proceed to implementation (outputs remediation plan for `/speckit:plan`)

### Offline Optimization

Convergence thresholds and recovery settings used by this command are a governed maintenance surface managed by the offline loop optimizer (042.004). The optimizer tunes deterministic numeric thresholds offline against real run traces and emits advisory-only candidate patches.

**Key constraints:**
- Optimization is offline only -- it does not run during live review sessions
- Promotion is advisory-only until replay fixtures and behavioral suites exist
- Prompt optimization is deferred future work (Phase 4b) and will use generated prompt packs, never direct agent markdown mutation

**References:**
- Optimizer configuration: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/optimizer-manifest.json`
- Tunable thresholds: `convergenceThreshold`, `stuckThreshold`, `maxIterations`, `compositeStopScore`
- Convergence reference: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md`

## 4. Next-Step Suggestions

### Examples

```
# Single-executor (default — unchanged)
/deep:review "skill:deep-research"
/deep:review:auto "specs/03--commands-and-skills/030-deep-research-review-mode/"
/deep:review:confirm "agent:deep-research" --max-iterations=5

# Fan-out: two CLI lineages in parallel
/deep:review:auto "skill:sk-code" \
  --executor=cli-opencode --model=o4-mini --label= \
  --executor=cli-claude-code --model=claude-opus-4-8 --config-dir=~/.claude-account2 --label=claude \
  --concurrency=2

# Fan-out: native + CLI (mixed)
/deep:review:auto "agent:deep-research" \
  --executor=native --count=1 --label=native \
  --executor=cli-opencode --model=o4-mini --count=2 \
  --concurrency=3
```

> **Review fan-out verdict:** strongest-restriction applies — if any lineage has an active P0 finding, the merged verdict is FAIL regardless of other lineages' outcomes.

### Next Steps

| Condition | Suggested Command | Reason |
|-----------|-------------------|--------|
| Review FAIL/CONDITIONAL, need fixes | `/speckit:plan [remediation]` | Plan remediation from review findings |
| Review PASS, ready for release | `/create:changelog` | Generate changelog entry |
| Need to fix specific findings | `/speckit:implement [spec-folder]` | Implement fixes from existing plan |
| Need more investigation | `/deep:research [topic]` | Deep research session for unclear areas |
| Want to refresh search support | `/memory:save [spec-folder]` | Refresh the indexed canonical spec document while canonical continuity stays in spec docs |

249	        command: "mkdir -p {state_paths.iteration_dir} && if [ ! -e {state_paths.config} ] && [ ! -e {state_paths.state_log} ] && [ ! -e {state_paths.strategy} ] && [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-strategy.md ] && ( [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-config.json ] || [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-state.jsonl ] ); then [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-config.json ] && mv {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-config.json {state_paths.config}; [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-state.jsonl ] && mv {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-state.jsonl {state_paths.state_log}; [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-strategy.md ] && mv {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-strategy.md {state_paths.strategy}; [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-dashboard.md ] && mv {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-dashboard.md {state_paths.dashboard}; find {spec_folder}/scratch -maxdepth 1 -name 'iteration-*.md' -exec mv {} {state_paths.iteration_dir}/ \\; ; [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/.deep-research-pause ] && mv {spec_folder}/scratch/.deep-research-pause {state_paths.pause_sentinel}; [ -e {state_paths.legacy_root_report} ] && mv {state_paths.legacy_root_report} {state_paths.review_output}; if [ -e {state_paths.state_log} ]; then printf '%s\n' '{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"migration\",\"mode\":\"review\",\"legacyArtifacts\":[\"deep-research-config.json\",\"deep-research-state.jsonl\",\".deep-research-pause\"],\"canonicalArtifacts\":[\"deep-review-config.json\",\"deep-review-state.jsonl\",\".deep-review-pause\"],\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\"}' >> {state_paths.state_log}; fi; fi; find {state_paths.local_packet_dir} -maxdepth 1 -name 'iteration-*.md' -exec mv {} {state_paths.iteration_dir}/ \\; 2>/dev/null || true"
250	
251	      step_acquire_lock:
252	        action: "Acquire the single-writer advisory lock before session classification"
253	        command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/loop-lock.cjs acquire --lock-path {state_paths.lock_file} --packet-id {spec_folder}"
254	        on_acquire: "Capture ownerPid from the JSON output and reuse it for every release below. If acquired=false, fail closed — another deep-review run already holds this packet."
255	        note: "Uses the promoted deep-loop-runtime loop-lock CLI (single source of truth: stale-lock reclaim, clean owner-scoped release). macOS/BSD locking is advisory — best-effort single-writer, not a hard mutex across host calls; stale-lock override is confirm-only or explicit recovery-only."
256	        # Lock-cleanup contract: the lock file MUST be released on every terminal exit path that follows acquisition.
257	        # Runtimes honoring step_acquire_lock are required to perform this cleanup before halting or pausing.
258	        on_halt:
259	          action: "Release the lock before any terminal halt branch returns control"
260	          applies_to: ["step_classify_session.on_invalid_state", "step_classify_session.on_completed_session"]
261	          command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/loop-lock.cjs release --lock-path {state_paths.lock_file} --owner-pid {captured_owner_pid}; failures here are logged but do not block the halt"
262	        on_cancel:
263	          action: "Release the lock on user cancel or pause-sentinel detection before sentinel processing"
264	          command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/loop-lock.cjs release --lock-path {state_paths.lock_file} --owner-pid {captured_owner_pid}; cancel completes regardless of cleanup result"
265	        on_workflow_exit:
266	          action: "Release the lock as the final step of any workflow exit path (success or failure)"
267	          command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/loop-lock.cjs release --lock-path {state_paths.lock_file} --owner-pid {captured_owner_pid}; idempotent — safe to invoke when already released"
268	
269	      step_classify_session:
270	        action: "Classify the session before writing files"
271	        inspect:
272	          - "{state_paths.config}"
273	          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
274	          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
275	          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
276	        classify:
277	          fresh: "None of the three state artifacts exist"
278	          resume: "Config, JSONL, and strategy all exist and config.mode == 'review' and agree on target/spec folder"
279	          completed-session: "Resume artifacts exist and config.status == 'complete'"
280	          invalid-state: "Any partial, missing, or contradictory combination"
281	        outputs:
282	          - session_state: "fresh | resume | completed-session | invalid-state"
283	        lifecycle_routing:
284	          - "If lineage_mode == restart and session_state is resume or completed-session, execute on_restart before on_resume or on_completed_session."
285	          - "If lineage_mode == auto or lineage_mode == resume, preserve the existing resume/completed/fresh behavior."
286	        on_resume:
287	          log: "Auto-resume: state artifacts are complete and consistent. Continue the same lineage (sessionId unchanged, generation unchanged, no archive)."
288	          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"resumed","mode":"review","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{session_id}","lineageMode":"resume","continuedFromRun":{iteration_count},"generation":{generation},"archivedPath":null,"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
289	          skip_to: phase_loop
290	        on_restart:
291	          action: "Archive the current review packet under the root-level review_archive/ tree and start a new lineage segment with a fresh sessionId and generation+1."
292	          command: "if [ -d {state_paths.packet_dir} ]; then mkdir -p {state_paths.archive_root} && mv {state_paths.packet_dir} {state_paths.archive_root}/{timestamp_slug}; fi"
293	          set:
294	            lineage_mode: "restart"
295	            parent_session_id: "{prior_session_id}"
296	            generation: "{prior_generation + 1}"
297	          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"restarted","mode":"review","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{prior_session_id}","lineageMode":"restart","generation":{generation},"archivedPath":"{state_paths.archive_root}/{timestamp_slug}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
298	          skip_to: step_create_config
299	        note_deferred_branches: "fork and completed-continue are deferred and no longer runtime-supported in this release. The reducer still reads `lineageMode` from config for dashboards and analysis, but only `new`, `resume`, and `restart` are live. See deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches for the one-session contract."
300	        on_completed_session:
301	          log: "Prior session already completed. Either archive the completed review/ tree and start a fresh session, or jump straight to synthesis with the existing artifacts."
302	          skip_to: phase_synthesis
303	        on_invalid_state:
304	          halt: true
305	          message: "Existing deep-review state in review/ is incomplete or contradictory. Repair or archive the invalid review packet before continuing."
306	        on_fresh:
307	          action: "Proceed with a new session"

309	      step_scope_discovery:
310	        action: "Resolve review target to a concrete file list based on target type"
311	        resolve:
312	          spec-folder: |
313	            1. Read spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md, checklist.md, implementation-summary.md
314	            2. Discover implementation files referenced in spec artifacts
315	            3. Discover test files associated with implementation
316	            4. Set scope = all discovered files
317	          skill: |
318	            1. Read SKILL.md from .opencode/skills/{skill_name}/
319	            2. Discover references/, assets/, scripts/ contents
320	            3. Find agent definitions referencing this skill across runtimes
321	            4. Find command entry points referencing this skill
322	            5. Set scope = all discovered files
323	          agent: |
324	            1. Find agent definition across repo-managed runtimes (.claude/agents/, .opencode/agents/, .agents/)
325	            2. Compare agent definitions for consistency
326	            3. Set scope = all discovered agent files
327	          track: |
328	            1. List all child spec folders under specs/{track}/ or .opencode/specs/{track}/
329	            2. For each: read spec.md + checklist.md
330	            3. Set scope = all child spec artifacts
331	          files: |
332	            1. Expand glob patterns from review_target
333	            2. Validate all files exist
334	            3. Discover immediate cross-references (imports, links)
335	            4. Set scope = expanded file list + cross-references
336	        outputs:
337	          - review_scope_files: "List of all files in review scope"
338	          - cross_reference_targets: "{ spec_paths: [...], code_paths: [...], test_paths: [...] }"
339	
340	      step_order_dimensions:
341	        action: "Order review dimensions based on risk priority"
342	        algorithm: |
343	          1. Iteration 0 (inventory pass): Build artifact map, identify file types, estimate complexity
344	          2. Subsequent iterations follow risk-ordered deep passes:
345	             correctness → security → traceability → maintainability
346	          3. If review_dimensions != "all", filter to only requested dimensions
347	          4. Derive traceability protocol plan:
348	             - core: spec_code, checklist_evidence
349	             - overlay: skill_agent, agent_cross_runtime, feature_catalog_code, playbook_capability
350	          5. If resource_map_present, reserve at least one traceability-oriented pass to cross-check target_files from applied/T-*.md against resource-map.md and classify touched / expected-by-scope / gap / absent-from-map outcomes
351	          6. Store ordered list as dimension_queue
352	        outputs:
353	          - dimension_queue: "Ordered list of dimensions to review"
354	          - inventory_pass: true

356	      step_normalize_review_dimensions:
357	        action: "Normalize reviewDimensions once during init for config + JSONL reuse"
358	        outputs:
359	          - review_dimensions_normalized: "Canonical string[] for deep-review-config.json"
360	          - review_dimensions_json: "JSON.stringify(review_dimensions_normalized) for JSONL config record"
361	        normalize: |
362	          Accept {review_dimensions} in any of these shapes and convert once:
363	            - "all" → ["correctness","security","traceability","maintainability"]
364	            - "correctness,security" → ["correctness","security"]
365	            - ["correctness"] → ["correctness"]
366	          Every element MUST be one of the four canonical dimension names.

374	        if_absent:

380	        template: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_config.json
381	        populate:
382	          topic: "Review: {review_target}"

385	          parentSessionId: null

441	      step_seed_coverage_graph:
442	        action: "Seed the review coverage graph before the first convergence check"
443	        transform: |
444	          1. Convert review_scope_files into FILE nodes.
445	          2. Convert review target groupings into coarse SLICE nodes only; do not expand SYMBOL
446	             or DEPENDENCY nodes in review mode.
447	          3. Emit COVERS edges from SLICE nodes to FILE nodes when the grouping is known.
448	          4. Bind coverage_seed_source to "scope_discovery" and coverage_seed_confidence to 0.50.

452	        outputs:

454	          - coverage_seed_edges_json: "Seed COVERS edges where review target groupings resolved"
455	          - coverage_seed_warning: "Warning emitted when the seed batch inserts zero nodes, else empty"

457	          log: "Coverage graph seed inserted zero nodes; continuing with an empty graph."
458	          proceed: true

461	        action: "Ensure Known Context is written only after strategy.md exists"
462	        condition: "prior_context_found"
463	        edit: "{state_paths.strategy}"
464	        note: "Append or replace the Known Context section with prior_context_summary and retained detail from prior_context"
465	
466	      step_enrich_strategy_resource_map:
467	        action: "Inject resource-map status into Known Context after strategy.md exists"
468	        edit: "{state_paths.strategy}"
469	        note: "When resource_map_present is true, append the resource_map_known_context snapshot and add Resource Map Coverage to the Review Charter. When false, append 'resource-map.md not present; skipping coverage gate'."
470	
471	      step_init_complete:
472	        log: "Initialization complete. Config, state, and strategy files created. Review scope: {review_scope_files_count} files, {dimension_queue_count} dimensions."
473	        set: { current_iteration: 1, stuck_count: 0, p0_count: 0, p1_count: 0, p2_count: 0 }

475	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
476	  # PHASE 2: ITERATION LOOP
477	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
478	  phase_loop:
479	    purpose: Dispatch review agent iterations until convergence
480	    repeat: true
481	    max_repeats: "{max_iterations}"
482	    steps:
483	
484	      step_read_state:
485	        action: "Read current state from JSONL, strategy, and config"
486	        read:
487	          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
488	          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
489	          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
490	          - "{state_paths.config}"
491	        extract:
492	          - iteration_count: "Count lines where type === 'iteration'"
493	          - current_iteration: "iteration_count + 1"
494	          - last_newFindingsRatio: "Extract newFindingsRatio from last iteration record"
495	          - last_focus: "Extract focus/dimension from latest iteration record, else 'none yet'"
496	          - ratio_prev: "Extract newFindingsRatio from second-latest iteration record, else 'N/A'"
497	          - ratio_latest: "Extract newFindingsRatio from latest iteration record, else 'N/A'"
498	          - next_dimension: "Next uncovered dimension from dimension_queue in strategy.md"
499	          - dimensions_covered: "List of dimensions already reviewed"
500	          - dimensions_remaining: "List of dimensions not yet reviewed"
501	          - dimension_coverage: "covered / total dimensions"
502	          - coverage_age: "Iterations since dimension coverage last changed; require >= 1 before STOP"
503	          - p0_count: "Count P0 findings across all iterations"
504	          - p1_count: "Count P1 findings across all iterations"
505	          - p2_count: "Count P2 findings across all iterations"
506	          - resource_map_present: "Extract boolean from deep-review-config.json, default false"
507	          - resource_map_prompt_line: "If resource_map_present is true, return 'Resource Map Coverage: cross-check target_files from {spec_folder}/applied/T-*.md against {spec_folder}/resource-map.md and classify only missed coverage as gaps.' Else return 'Resource Map Coverage: resource-map.md not present; skipping coverage gate.'"
508	          - session_id: "Extract sessionId from deep-review-config.json"
509	          - generation: "Extract generation from deep-review-config.json, else 1"
510	          - last_3_summaries: "Summarize the latest 3 iteration records as 'run N: dimension (ratio, P0/P1/P2)'"
511	          - latest_status: "Extract status from latest iteration record, else 'none yet'"
512	          - last_claim_adjudication_passed: "Scan state.jsonl for the most recent `claim_adjudication` event; use its `passed` boolean. Default true when no event exists or when activeP0 + activeP1 == 0 (gate is vacuous without adjudicable findings)."
513	          - claim_adjudication_active_count: "Count of active P0/P1 findings at the time of the last claim_adjudication event; 0 when none exist"
514	          - security_sensitive_fix_scope: "Boolean true when the current review is a rerun after fixes involving security, path disclosure, auth/authz, sandboxing, env precedence, public schemas, persistence, or user-visible error payloads; derive from the latest review report, remediation notes, or planning packet when present, else false."
515	          - fix_completeness_replay_required_rows: "Count prior active or remediated P0/P1 findings, plus any prior security/path P2 findings, that require closed-gate replay before STOP."
516	          - fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows: "Count required replay rows marked PASS with file:line or command evidence and producer/consumer/matrix coverage."
517	          - stop_policy: "Extract stopPolicy from deep-review-config.json, default 'convergence'. Valid values: convergence, max-iterations."
518	
519	      step_check_pause_sentinel:
520	        action: "Check for pause sentinel file"
521	        check: "Does {state_paths.pause_sentinel} exist?"
522	        if_exists:
523	          set:
524	            pending_stop_event: "paused"
525	            pending_stop_reason: "userPaused"
526	            pending_stop_detail: "sentinel file detected"
527	          skip_to: step_normalize_pause_events
528	        if_not_exists:
529	          proceed: true
530	
531	      step_graph_convergence:
532	        action: "Evaluate coverage-graph convergence before the inline review convergence vote"
533	        inspect:
534	          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
535	          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
536	        transform: |
537	          1. Call graph convergence on the current review lineage before the inline 3-signal vote.
538	          2. Record the decision as one of CONTINUE, STOP_ALLOWED, STOP_BLOCKED.
539	          3. Preserve raw graph signals and blockers for reducer rollup and blocked-stop evidence.
540	          4. Normalize blockers to both JSON array and CSV forms for downstream stop handling.
541	          5. Set graph_stop_blocked = true only when graph_decision == "STOP_BLOCKED".
542	        bash: 'node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/convergence.cjs --spec-folder "{spec_folder}" --loop-type "review" --session-id "{session_id}"'
543	        note: "Invoke the runtime script directly. Review-side graph convergence must run before the inline review stop vote."
544	        append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"graph_convergence","mode":"review","run":{current_iteration},"decision":"{graph_decision}","signals":{graph_signals_json},"blockers":{graph_blockers_json},"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{session_id}","generation":{generation}}'
545	        outputs:
546	          - graph_decision: "CONTINUE | STOP_ALLOWED | STOP_BLOCKED"
547	          - graph_signals_json: "JSON object returned by convergence.cjs"
548	          - graph_blockers_json: "JSON array of graph blockers when present, else []"
549	          - graph_blockers_csv: "Comma-separated blocker summary when graph blocks STOP, else empty string"
550	          - graph_stop_blocked: "Boolean true when graph_decision == STOP_BLOCKED"
551	
552	      step_check_convergence:
553	        action: "Evaluate graph-assisted review stop conditions and the legal-stop decision tree"
554	        algorithm: |
555	          0. CLAIM-ADJUDICATION PRE-CHECK (universal STOP veto; runs before hard stops and composite convergence):
556	             claim_adjudication_gate_pass = (last_claim_adjudication_passed != false) OR (p0_count + p1_count == 0)
557	             If claim_adjudication_gate_pass == false:
558	               candidate_decision = "BLOCKED"
559	               stop_blocked = true
560	               stopReason = "blockedStop"
561	               blocked_gates = ["claimAdjudicationGate"]
562	               recovery_strategy = "Reviewer must add typed claim-adjudication packets (claim, evidenceRefs, counterevidenceSought, alternativeExplanation, finalSeverity, confidence, downgradeTrigger) for every active P0/P1 finding before STOP is allowed."
563	               Skip hard stops (1) and composite convergence (4); proceed directly to step 5 legal-stop decision-tree assembly so blocked_by_json, gate outputs, and the blocked_stop event still populate consistently.
564	          1. HARD STOPS:
565	             a) if iteration_count >= max_iterations: set decision = "STOP", stopReason = "maxIterationsReached", hard_stop_reason = "max_iterations_reached", preserve any failed legal-stop gates as terminal evidence, and proceed to synthesis without legal-stop veto
566	             b) if stop_policy == "max-iterations" AND iteration_count < max_iterations: do not allow all-dimensions-clean or composite convergence to stop; treat convergence signals as telemetry, set decision = "CONTINUE", and broaden the next review angle.
567	             c) if all dimensions clean AND required traceability protocols are covered AND p0_count == 0 AND p1_count == 0 AND coverage_age >= 1 AND all quality gates pass (evidence: every P0/P1 has file:line; scope: all findings within target; coverage: all dimensions reviewed): mark hard_stop_reason = "all_dimensions_clean" and promote to STOP candidate
568	             Operator stop reasons (`maxIterationsReached`, `manualStop`, `userPaused`, unrecoverable `error`) are terminal ceilings. They do not hide failed gates; they record them in synthesis and stop dispatch.
569	          2. STUCK DETECTION (threshold 2):
570	             if stuck_count >= 2: STUCK_RECOVERY
571	          3. GRAPH CONVERGENCE PREREQUISITE:
572	               graph_decision, graph_signals_json, and graph_blockers_json are already populated by step_graph_convergence.
573	               Final STOP is never legal unless the inline review vote says STOP AND graph_decision == "STOP_ALLOWED".
574	               If graph_decision == "STOP_BLOCKED", set stop_blocked = true and route through blocked_stop emission instead of stopping.
575	          4. COMPOSITE CONVERGENCE (3-signal weighted vote with split thresholds):
576	               signals = []
577	               a) Rolling average (w=0.30, window=2, needs 2+ iterations):
578	                  avg = mean(last 2 newFindingsRatios)
579	                  stop if avg <= 0.08
580	               b) MAD noise floor (w=0.25, needs 3+ iterations):
581	                  noiseFloor = MAD(all ratios) * 1.4826
582	                  stop if latest ratio <= noiseFloor
583	               c) Dimension coverage (w=0.45):
584	                  coverage = dimensions_covered / total_dimensions
585	                  stop if coverage >= 1.0 AND required traceability protocols are covered AND coverage_age >= 1
586	               Redistribute weights for unavailable signals.
587	               if latest ratio <= 0.05: mark no_progress_threshold_reached
588	               if stop_policy == "max-iterations" AND iteration_count < max_iterations: convergence votes are telemetry only; set weighted_stop_score for diagnostics, keep decision = "CONTINUE", and choose a broader next focus.
589	               else if weighted_stop_score >= 0.60: inline STOP becomes a candidate and binary gates must run
590	          5. LEGAL-STOP DECISION TREE (review-specific gates; runs whenever step 0, all-dimensions-clean, or the composite vote promotes STOP to a candidate; terminal max-iteration/manual/error stops bypass veto and record failed gates as evidence):
591	               a) convergenceGate: pass when a hard-stop condition is met OR weighted_stop_score >= 0.60; record score
592	               b) dimensionCoverageGate: pass when configured dimensions and required traceability coverage are complete and coverage_age >= 1; record covered and missing dimensions
593	               c) p0ResolutionGate: pass when activeP0 == 0; record activeP0
594	               d) evidenceDensityGate: pass when every active P0/P1 has concrete file:line evidence and no active P0/P1 relies only on inference; record avgEvidencePerFinding
595	               e) hotspotSaturationGate: pass when review hotspots have been revisited enough times to satisfy the saturation heuristic
596	               f) claimAdjudicationGate: pass when claim_adjudication_gate_pass == true (i.e. last_claim_adjudication_passed != false OR there are no active P0/P1 findings to adjudicate); record last_claim_adjudication_passed and claim_adjudication_active_count
597	               g) fixCompletenessReplayGate: pass when security_sensitive_fix_scope == false OR every required closed-gate replay row passes with file:line or command evidence and producer/consumer/matrix coverage; record security_sensitive_fix_scope, fix_completeness_replay_required_rows, and fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows
598	               h) candidateCoverageGate: skip entirely when `reviewDepthSchemaVersion` is not 2 on the latest iteration. Otherwise pass = false; record `searchDebt` items as blocked-stop evidence; blocker name = "candidateCoverageGate"; recovery_strategy = "Resolve deferred/blocked obligations in next iteration or downgrade scope explicitly to trivial with cited evidence."
