# DEEP REVIEW: AUTONOMOUS DEEP REVIEW LOOP (AUTO MODE)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DEEP REVIEW: AUTONOMOUS DEEP REVIEW LOOP (AUTO MODE)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
role: Deep Review Loop Manager
purpose: Iterative code/spec review with convergence detection across review dimensions
action: Run deep-review loop from initialization through synthesis
operating_mode:
  workflow: iterative_loop
  workflow_compliance: MANDATORY
  workflow_execution: autonomous
  approvals: none
  tracking: progressive_review_state
  validation: convergence_detection

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# OPERATING PRINCIPLES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
operating_principles:
  core: "Fresh context per iteration + externalized state = no context degradation"
  approach: "Dispatch focused review agent per dimension, evaluate convergence, repeat or stop"
  mandate: "Never hold findings in memory; write everything to files. Review target is READ-ONLY."

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# USER INPUTS (from setup phase)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
user_inputs:
  review_target: "[REVIEW_TARGET] - What to review. REQUIRED."
  review_target_type: "[spec-folder|skill|agent|track|files] - Target type for scope derivation. REQUIRED."
  review_dimensions: "[all|correctness,security,traceability,maintainability] - Dimensions to check. Default: all."
  spec_folder: "[SPEC_FOLDER] - Spec folder path for state files. REQUIRED."
  session_id: "[SESSION_ID] - Optional caller-supplied session id for fan-out lineages. Default: timestamp."
  lineage_mode: "[auto|resume|restart] - Session lifecycle intent. Default: auto. The runtime only persists lineage events for `resume` (same sessionId, no archive) and `restart` (new sessionId, generation+1, prior review/ archived). `fork` and `completed-continue` are deferred and no longer accepted — see deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches."
  max_iterations: 7
  convergence_threshold: 0.10
  stop_policy: "[convergence|max-iterations] - Stop behavior. Default: convergence. max-iterations treats convergence as telemetry until the hard iteration ceiling."
  config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir: "[optional internal path] - Fan-out lineage artifact override for command-owned single-lineage execution."

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# CONTEXT LOADING
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
context_loading:
  trigger: "At workflow START, before phase_init"
  purpose: "Load prior review context for the target and buffer it until strategy.md exists"
  mcp_integration:
    tool: memory_context
    note: "Call MCP tools directly - NEVER through Code Mode"
    parameters: { input: "{review_target}", mode: "focused", intent: "understand", includeContent: true }
  when_to_skip: ["User says 'skip context' or 'fresh start'"]
  behavior:
    if_context_found: "Store full results as prior_context plus a concise prior_context_summary; inject after strategy.md is created."
    if_no_context: "Set prior_context and prior_context_summary to 'None' and continue."

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SKILL REFERENCE
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
skill_reference:
  skill: deep-loop-workflows
  skill_md: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/SKILL.md
  references:
    loop_protocol: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md
    state_format: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/references/state/state_format.md
    convergence: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/references/convergence/convergence.md
  templates:
    config: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_config.json
    strategy: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_strategy.md

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# AGENT CONFIGURATION
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
agent_config:
  agent: deep-review
  agent_file: ".opencode/agents/deep-review.md"
  # Canonical path. Runtime-specific directories (e.g. .claude/agents/) reference this source.
  mode: subagent
  model: opus
  leaf_only: true
  tools: [Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob]
  mcp_servers: [mk-spec-memory]
  tool_call_budget: { target: 9, soft_max: 12, hard_max: 13 }

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STATE FILE PATHS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
state_paths:
  # Template paths document the canonical packet layout. Runtime resolution happens via
  # step_resolve_artifact_root plus the shared resolver, so root specs keep
  # root-local review/ and child phases keep local review/ packet dirs.
  # Canonical root-spec write targets remain review/deep-review-config.json,
  # review/deep-review-state.jsonl, and review/deep-review-findings-registry.json.
  packet_dir: "{artifact_dir}"
  local_packet_dir: "{spec_folder}/review"
  legacy_root_report: "{spec_folder}/review-report.md"
  config: "{artifact_dir}/deep-review-config.json"
  state_log: "{artifact_dir}/deep-review-state.jsonl"
  findings_registry: "{artifact_dir}/deep-review-findings-registry.json"
  strategy: "{artifact_dir}/deep-review-strategy.md"
  dashboard: "{artifact_dir}/deep-review-dashboard.md"
  pause_sentinel: "{artifact_dir}/.deep-review-pause"
  lock_file: "{artifact_dir}/.deep-review.lock"
  prompt_dir: "{artifact_dir}/prompts"
  iteration_dir: "{artifact_dir}/iterations"
  iteration_pattern: "{artifact_dir}/iterations/iteration-{NNN}.md"
  # Per-iteration structured delta stream — complement to the canonical state_log
  # append. MUST contain at least one record with `type === "iteration"`; variants
  # like `"iteration_delta"` are silently dropped by the reducer.
  delta_dir: "{artifact_dir}/deltas"
  delta_pattern: "{artifact_dir}/deltas/iter-{NNN}.jsonl"
  review_output: "{artifact_dir}/review-report.md"
  resource_map_output: "{artifact_dir}/resource-map.md"
  archive_root: "{artifact_archive_root}"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# WORKFLOW
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
workflow:

  step_resolve_artifact_root:
    description: "Resolve artifact directory — use fan-out lineage override when provided, else resolve from spec_folder"
    note: "Root specs keep review artifacts directly in {spec_folder}/review/. Child phases and sub-phases keep local packet directories in {spec_folder}/review/{packet}/, and archives stay local at {spec_folder}/review_archive/{packet}/."
    branch_on: "config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir"
    if_present:
      bind:
        artifact_dir: "{config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir}"
        artifact_root_dir: "{config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir}"
        artifact_subfolder: null
        artifact_archive_root: "{config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir}/_archive"
    if_absent:
      command: "node -e \"const { resolveArtifactRoot } = require('.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/shared/review-research-paths.cjs'); console.log(JSON.stringify(resolveArtifactRoot('{spec_folder}', 'review')));\""
      outputs:
        - artifact_root_dir: "Resolved local review owner directory"
        - artifact_subfolder: "Local packet subfolder ({ownerSlug}-pt-{NN}), else null"
        - artifact_dir: "Resolved review packet directory for this target"
        - artifact_archive_root: "Resolved local review archive directory for this target"

  step_apply_lifecycle_request:
    description: "Honor explicit auto-mode restart before fan-out or normal initialization"
    skip_when: "lineage_mode != 'restart'"
    instructions: |
      This is the autonomous equivalent of the confirm-mode restart choice. Run it
      immediately after artifact resolution and before fan-out so old packet files
      cannot be merged with fresh lineage outputs. A restart request must never
      fall back to auto-resume. In auto mode, the explicit restart flag is operator
      authorization to archive the resolved review packet, so do not ask for a
      second confirmation. Rollback is to move the timestamped archive back to
      the packet directory.
    command: "if [ -d \"{state_paths.packet_dir}\" ]; then mkdir -p \"{state_paths.archive_root}\" && mv \"{state_paths.packet_dir}\" \"{state_paths.archive_root}/{timestamp_slug}\"; fi; mkdir -p \"{state_paths.packet_dir}\""
    outputs:
      - archived_path: "{state_paths.archive_root}/{timestamp_slug} when a prior packet existed, else null"
      - lineage_mode: "restart"
    on_fail: "Halt with STATUS=FAIL; do not continue as resume or auto."

  step_fanout_spawn:
    description: "Spawn fan-out lineages; skip and proceed to phase_init normally when single-executor"
    skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
    instructions: |
      Fan-out mode is active. Spawn all lineages. Each lineage runs the full loop in its own
      isolated sub-packet under {artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/.
      A cli-opencode lineage inside this fan-out is an explicit parallel-detached OpenCode
      session with a separate lineage directory and session id; it is not the single-executor
      self-invocation path. fanout-run.cjs owns the stack-only recursion guard for that
      first-level detached lineage and still blocks recursive same-kind dispatch from inside it.
      fanout-run.cjs owns native and CLI lineage execution; native is run through the
      deep/review command surface with config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir so the LEAF
      deep-review agent is used only for individual iterations, never as a full-loop
      sub-agent or pasted phase-running prompt.
      After all lineages complete, jump directly to phase_synthesis — skip phase_init and
      phase_main_loop entirely (those are the single-executor path). The merge step in
      phase_synthesis will consolidate results across all lineages.
    sub_steps:
      step_fanout_spawn_cli:
        description: "Spawn all native and CLI lineages in capped worker pool"
        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors"
        command: |
          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs \
            --spec-folder {spec_folder} \
            --loop-type review \
            --fanout-config-json '{config.fanout_json}' \
            --base-artifact-dir {artifact_dir} \
            --convergence-threshold {convergence_threshold} \
            --stop-policy {stop_policy}
        working_directory: "{repo_root}"
        wait_for_completion: true

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # PHASE 1: INITIALIZATION
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  phase_init:
    purpose: Create all state files for the review session and resolve review scope
    steps:

      step_preflight_contract:
        action: "Validate required setup bindings and executable workflow contract before any file writes"
        verify:
          - required_values_present: [review_target, review_target_type, review_dimensions, spec_folder, lineage_mode, max_iterations, convergence_threshold, stop_policy]
          - spec_folder_is_within: "specs/ or .opencode/specs/ (canonicalize alias roots before validation)"
          - unresolved_placeholders: "FAIL if any user-facing template references placeholders not produced by earlier steps"
          - tool_contract: "FAIL if executable steps reference tools outside the command allowlist"
        on_fail: "Halt with STATUS=FAIL and report the missing binding or unsupported contract reference"

      step_create_directories:
        action: "Create spec folder and canonical review packet directories if they don't exist"
        command: "mkdir -p {state_paths.prompt_dir} {state_paths.iteration_dir} {state_paths.delta_dir}"

      step_detect_resource_map:
        action: "Detect resource-map.md before config creation so init can persist coverage-gate state"
        inspect:
          - "{spec_folder}/resource-map.md"
        classify:
          present: "resource-map.md exists at init"
          absent: "resource-map.md does not exist at init"
        outputs:
          - resource_map_present: "Boolean true when {spec_folder}/resource-map.md exists, else false"
          - resource_map_known_context: "When present: concise resource-map snapshot for Known Context and charter framing. When absent: 'resource-map.md not present; skipping coverage gate'."

      step_migrate_legacy_review_state:
        action: "Rehome legacy review artifacts into review/iterations before session classification"
        detect:
          canonical_review_state:
            - "{state_paths.config}"
            - "{state_paths.state_log}"
            - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
            - "{state_paths.strategy}"
          legacy_review_state:
            - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-config.json"
            - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-state.jsonl"
            - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-strategy.md"
            - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-dashboard.md"
            - "{spec_folder}/scratch/iteration-*.md"
            - "{spec_folder}/scratch/.deep-research-pause"
            - "{state_paths.local_packet_dir}/iteration-*.md"
            - "{state_paths.legacy_root_report}"
          legacy_review_markers:
            - "scratch/deep-review-strategy.md exists"
            - "scratch/deep-research-config.json declares mode == 'review' OR scratch/deep-research-state.jsonl contains mode == 'review'"
        migrate_only:
          - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-config.json -> {state_paths.config}"
          - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-state.jsonl -> {state_paths.state_log}"
          - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-strategy.md -> {state_paths.strategy}"
          - "{spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-dashboard.md -> {state_paths.dashboard}"
          - "{spec_folder}/scratch/iteration-*.md -> {state_paths.iteration_dir}/"
          - "{spec_folder}/scratch/.deep-research-pause -> {state_paths.pause_sentinel}"
          - "{state_paths.local_packet_dir}/iteration-*.md -> {state_paths.iteration_dir}/"
          - "{state_paths.legacy_root_report} -> {state_paths.review_output}"
        on_canonical_present: "Leave canonical review/ state untouched and do not overwrite it with legacy scratch artifacts."
        on_legacy_detected: "Move only the review-specific whitelist, append a migration event to the canonical JSONL, and leave unrelated scratch files untouched."
        on_conflict: "If canonical review/ state and legacy scratch review state both exist but disagree, halt for operator repair instead of merging contradictory state."
        command: "mkdir -p {state_paths.iteration_dir} && if [ ! -e {state_paths.config} ] && [ ! -e {state_paths.state_log} ] && [ ! -e {state_paths.strategy} ] && [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-strategy.md ] && ( [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-config.json ] || [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-state.jsonl ] ); then [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-config.json ] && mv {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-config.json {state_paths.config}; [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-state.jsonl ] && mv {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-research-state.jsonl {state_paths.state_log}; [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-strategy.md ] && mv {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-strategy.md {state_paths.strategy}; [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-dashboard.md ] && mv {spec_folder}/scratch/deep-review-dashboard.md {state_paths.dashboard}; find {spec_folder}/scratch -maxdepth 1 -name 'iteration-*.md' -exec mv {} {state_paths.iteration_dir}/ \\; ; [ -e {spec_folder}/scratch/.deep-research-pause ] && mv {spec_folder}/scratch/.deep-research-pause {state_paths.pause_sentinel}; [ -e {state_paths.legacy_root_report} ] && mv {state_paths.legacy_root_report} {state_paths.review_output}; if [ -e {state_paths.state_log} ]; then printf '%s\n' '{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"migration\",\"mode\":\"review\",\"legacyArtifacts\":[\"deep-research-config.json\",\"deep-research-state.jsonl\",\".deep-research-pause\"],\"canonicalArtifacts\":[\"deep-review-config.json\",\"deep-review-state.jsonl\",\".deep-review-pause\"],\"timestamp\":\"{ISO_8601_NOW}\"}' >> {state_paths.state_log}; fi; fi; find {state_paths.local_packet_dir} -maxdepth 1 -name 'iteration-*.md' -exec mv {} {state_paths.iteration_dir}/ \\; 2>/dev/null || true"

      step_acquire_lock:
        action: "Acquire the single-writer advisory lock before session classification"
        command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/loop-lock.cjs acquire --lock-path {state_paths.lock_file} --packet-id {spec_folder}"
        on_acquire: "Capture ownerPid from the JSON output and reuse it for every release below. If acquired=false, fail closed — another deep-review run already holds this packet."
        note: "Uses the promoted deep-loop-runtime loop-lock CLI (single source of truth: stale-lock reclaim, clean owner-scoped release). macOS/BSD locking is advisory — best-effort single-writer, not a hard mutex across host calls; stale-lock override is confirm-only or explicit recovery-only."
        # Lock-cleanup contract: the lock file MUST be released on every terminal exit path that follows acquisition.
        # Runtimes honoring step_acquire_lock are required to perform this cleanup before halting or pausing.
        on_halt:
          action: "Release the lock before any terminal halt branch returns control"
          applies_to: ["step_classify_session.on_invalid_state", "step_classify_session.on_completed_session"]
          command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/loop-lock.cjs release --lock-path {state_paths.lock_file} --owner-pid {captured_owner_pid}; failures here are logged but do not block the halt"
        on_cancel:
          action: "Release the lock on user cancel or pause-sentinel detection before sentinel processing"
          command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/loop-lock.cjs release --lock-path {state_paths.lock_file} --owner-pid {captured_owner_pid}; cancel completes regardless of cleanup result"
        on_workflow_exit:
          action: "Release the lock as the final step of any workflow exit path (success or failure)"
          command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/loop-lock.cjs release --lock-path {state_paths.lock_file} --owner-pid {captured_owner_pid}; idempotent — safe to invoke when already released"

      step_classify_session:
        action: "Classify the session before writing files"
        inspect:
          - "{state_paths.config}"
          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
        classify:
          fresh: "None of the three state artifacts exist"
          resume: "Config, JSONL, and strategy all exist and config.mode == 'review' and agree on target/spec folder"
          completed-session: "Resume artifacts exist and config.status == 'complete'"
          invalid-state: "Any partial, missing, or contradictory combination"
        outputs:
          - session_state: "fresh | resume | completed-session | invalid-state"
        lifecycle_routing:
          - "If lineage_mode == restart and session_state is resume or completed-session, execute on_restart before on_resume or on_completed_session."
          - "If lineage_mode == auto or lineage_mode == resume, preserve the existing resume/completed/fresh behavior."
        on_resume:
          log: "Auto-resume: state artifacts are complete and consistent. Continue the same lineage (sessionId unchanged, generation unchanged, no archive)."
          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"resumed","mode":"review","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{session_id}","lineageMode":"resume","continuedFromRun":{iteration_count},"generation":{generation},"archivedPath":null,"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
          skip_to: phase_loop
        on_restart:
          action: "Archive the current review packet under the root-level review_archive/ tree and start a new lineage segment with a fresh sessionId and generation+1."
          command: "if [ -d {state_paths.packet_dir} ]; then mkdir -p {state_paths.archive_root} && mv {state_paths.packet_dir} {state_paths.archive_root}/{timestamp_slug}; fi"
          set:
            lineage_mode: "restart"
            parent_session_id: "{prior_session_id}"
            generation: "{prior_generation + 1}"
          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"restarted","mode":"review","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{prior_session_id}","lineageMode":"restart","generation":{generation},"archivedPath":"{state_paths.archive_root}/{timestamp_slug}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
          skip_to: step_create_config
        note_deferred_branches: "fork and completed-continue are deferred and no longer runtime-supported in this release. The reducer still reads `lineageMode` from config for dashboards and analysis, but only `new`, `resume`, and `restart` are live. See deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches for the one-session contract."
        on_completed_session:
          log: "Prior session already completed. Either archive the completed review/ tree and start a fresh session, or jump straight to synthesis with the existing artifacts."
          skip_to: phase_synthesis
        on_invalid_state:
          halt: true
          message: "Existing deep-review state in review/ is incomplete or contradictory. Repair or archive the invalid review packet before continuing."
        on_fresh:
          action: "Proceed with a new session"

      step_scope_discovery:
        action: "Resolve review target to a concrete file list based on target type"
        resolve:
          spec-folder: |
            1. Read spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md, checklist.md, implementation-summary.md
            2. Discover implementation files referenced in spec artifacts
            3. Discover test files associated with implementation
            4. Set scope = all discovered files
          skill: |
            1. Read SKILL.md from .opencode/skills/{skill_name}/
            2. Discover references/, assets/, scripts/ contents
            3. Find agent definitions referencing this skill across runtimes
            4. Find command entry points referencing this skill
            5. Set scope = all discovered files
          agent: |
            1. Find agent definition across repo-managed runtimes (.claude/agents/, .opencode/agents/, .agents/)
            2. Compare agent definitions for consistency
            3. Set scope = all discovered agent files
          track: |
            1. List all child spec folders under specs/{track}/ or .opencode/specs/{track}/
            2. For each: read spec.md + checklist.md
            3. Set scope = all child spec artifacts
          files: |
            1. Expand glob patterns from review_target
            2. Validate all files exist
            3. Discover immediate cross-references (imports, links)
            4. Set scope = expanded file list + cross-references
        outputs:
          - review_scope_files: "List of all files in review scope"
          - cross_reference_targets: "{ spec_paths: [...], code_paths: [...], test_paths: [...] }"

      step_order_dimensions:
        action: "Order review dimensions based on risk priority"
        algorithm: |
          1. Iteration 0 (inventory pass): Build artifact map, identify file types, estimate complexity
          2. Subsequent iterations follow risk-ordered deep passes:
             correctness → security → traceability → maintainability
          3. If review_dimensions != "all", filter to only requested dimensions
          4. Derive traceability protocol plan:
             - core: spec_code, checklist_evidence
             - overlay: skill_agent, agent_cross_runtime, feature_catalog_code, playbook_capability
          5. If resource_map_present, reserve at least one traceability-oriented pass to cross-check target_files from applied/T-*.md against resource-map.md and classify touched / expected-by-scope / gap / absent-from-map outcomes
          6. Store ordered list as dimension_queue
        outputs:
          - dimension_queue: "Ordered list of dimensions to review"
          - inventory_pass: true

      step_normalize_review_dimensions:
        action: "Normalize reviewDimensions once during init for config + JSONL reuse"
        outputs:
          - review_dimensions_normalized: "Canonical string[] for deep-review-config.json"
          - review_dimensions_json: "JSON.stringify(review_dimensions_normalized) for JSONL config record"
        normalize: |
          Accept {review_dimensions} in any of these shapes and convert once:
            - "all" → ["correctness","security","traceability","maintainability"]
            - "correctness,security" → ["correctness","security"]
            - ["correctness"] → ["correctness"]
          Every element MUST be one of the four canonical dimension names.

      step_resolve_session_id:
        action: "Resolve init session id from caller when supplied, else preserve timestamp fallback"
        branch_on: "session_id"
        if_present:
          bind:
            session_id_init: "{session_id}"
        if_absent:
          bind:
            session_id_init: "{ISO_8601_NOW}"

      step_create_config:
        action: "Create deep-review-config.json from template with review-specific fields"
        template: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_config.json
        populate:
          topic: "Review: {review_target}"
          mode: "review"
          sessionId: "{session_id_init}"
          parentSessionId: null
          lineageMode: "new"
          generation: 1
          continuedFromRun: null
          reviewTarget: "{review_target}"
          reviewTargetType: "{review_target_type}"
          # Reuse the single normalized init-time value from step_normalize_review_dimensions.
          reviewDimensions: "{review_dimensions_normalized}"
          resource_map_present: "{resource_map_present}"
          reviewScopeFiles: "{review_scope_files}"
          severityThreshold: "P2"
          crossReference:
            core: ["spec_code", "checklist_evidence"]
            overlay: ["skill_agent", "agent_cross_runtime", "feature_catalog_code", "playbook_capability"]
          maxIterations: "{max_iterations}"
          # Semantic: weighted P0/P1/P2 severity ratio. See SKILL.md §"Convergence Threshold Semantics".
          convergenceThreshold: "{convergence_threshold}"
          stopPolicy: "{stop_policy}"
          stuckThreshold: 2
          maxDurationMinutes: 120
          specFolder: "{spec_folder}"
          # Honor the parsed --no-resource-map flag from deep-review.md:71 (default true; false when flag is set).
          # The markdown command parses $ARGUMENTS and binds {resource_map_emit} before this step runs.
          resource_map.emit: "{resource_map_emit}"
          createdAt: "{ISO_8601_NOW}"
          status: "initialized"
          releaseReadinessState: "in-progress"
          executionMode: "auto"
        output: "{state_paths.config}"

      step_create_state_log:
        action: "Initialize JSONL state log with config record including review fields and lineage metadata"
        note: "Reuse review_dimensions_json from step_normalize_review_dimensions. Never re-normalize or wrap the raw template value."
        # Same parsed --no-resource-map flag honored here so config and state_log agree from line 1.
        content: '{"type":"config","mode":"review","reviewTarget":"{review_target}","reviewTargetType":"{review_target_type}","reviewDimensions":{review_dimensions_json},"resource_map_present":{resource_map_present},"resource_map":{"emit":{resource_map_emit}},"sessionId":"{session_id_init}","parentSessionId":null,"lineageMode":"new","generation":1,"continuedFromRun":null,"maxIterations":{max_iterations},"convergenceThreshold":{convergence_threshold},"stopPolicy":"{stop_policy}","createdAt":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","specFolder":"{spec_folder}"}'
        output: "{state_paths.state_log}"

      step_create_findings_registry:
        action: "Initialize reducer-owned review findings registry"
        content: '{"sessionId":"{session_id_init}","generation":1,"lineageMode":"new","openFindings":[],"resolvedFindings":[],"repeatedFindings":[],"dimensionCoverage":{"correctness":false,"security":false,"traceability":false,"maintainability":false},"findingsBySeverity":{"P0":0,"P1":0,"P2":0},"openFindingsCount":0,"resolvedFindingsCount":0,"convergenceScore":0.0}'
        output: "{state_paths.findings_registry}"

      step_create_strategy:
        action: "Create review strategy file from deep_review_strategy.md template"
        template: .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_strategy.md
        populate:
          review_target: "{review_target}"
          review_target_type: "{review_target_type}"
          dimensions: "{dimension_queue}"
          scope_files: "{review_scope_files}"
          cross_reference_targets: "{cross_reference_targets}"
          max_iterations: "{max_iterations}"
          convergence_threshold: "{convergence_threshold}"
          stop_policy: "{stop_policy}"
        output: "{state_paths.strategy}"

      step_seed_coverage_graph:
        action: "Seed the review coverage graph before the first convergence check"
        transform: |
          1. Convert review_scope_files into FILE nodes.
          2. Convert review target groupings into coarse SLICE nodes only; do not expand SYMBOL
             or DEPENDENCY nodes in review mode.
          3. Emit COVERS edges from SLICE nodes to FILE nodes when the grouping is known.
          4. Bind coverage_seed_source to "scope_discovery" and coverage_seed_confidence to 0.50.
          5. When scope discovery yields no files, emit [] for both arrays; the upsert script
             returns a warning and review continues with an empty graph.
        bash: "node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/upsert.cjs --spec-folder \"{spec_folder}\" --loop-type \"review\" --session-id \"{config.sessionId}\" --nodes '{coverage_seed_nodes_json}' --edges '{coverage_seed_edges_json}' --seed-source \"{coverage_seed_source}\" --seed-confidence \"{coverage_seed_confidence}\""
        outputs:
          - coverage_seed_nodes_json: "Seed nodes for review SLICE and FILE kinds only"
          - coverage_seed_edges_json: "Seed COVERS edges where review target groupings resolved"
          - coverage_seed_warning: "Warning emitted when the seed batch inserts zero nodes, else empty"
        on_warning:
          log: "Coverage graph seed inserted zero nodes; continuing with an empty graph."
          proceed: true

      step_enrich_strategy_context:
        action: "Ensure Known Context is written only after strategy.md exists"
        condition: "prior_context_found"
        edit: "{state_paths.strategy}"
        note: "Append or replace the Known Context section with prior_context_summary and retained detail from prior_context"

      step_enrich_strategy_resource_map:
        action: "Inject resource-map status into Known Context after strategy.md exists"
        edit: "{state_paths.strategy}"
        note: "When resource_map_present is true, append the resource_map_known_context snapshot and add Resource Map Coverage to the Review Charter. When false, append 'resource-map.md not present; skipping coverage gate'."

      step_init_complete:
        log: "Initialization complete. Config, state, and strategy files created. Review scope: {review_scope_files_count} files, {dimension_queue_count} dimensions."
        set: { current_iteration: 1, stuck_count: 0, p0_count: 0, p1_count: 0, p2_count: 0 }

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # PHASE 2: ITERATION LOOP
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  phase_loop:
    purpose: Dispatch review agent iterations until convergence
    repeat: true
    max_repeats: "{max_iterations}"
    steps:

      step_read_state:
        action: "Read current state from JSONL, strategy, and config"
        read:
          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
          - "{state_paths.config}"
        extract:
          - iteration_count: "Count lines where type === 'iteration'"
          - current_iteration: "iteration_count + 1"
          - last_newFindingsRatio: "Extract newFindingsRatio from last iteration record"
          - last_focus: "Extract focus/dimension from latest iteration record, else 'none yet'"
          - ratio_prev: "Extract newFindingsRatio from second-latest iteration record, else 'N/A'"
          - ratio_latest: "Extract newFindingsRatio from latest iteration record, else 'N/A'"
          - next_dimension: "Next uncovered dimension from dimension_queue in strategy.md"
          - dimensions_covered: "List of dimensions already reviewed"
          - dimensions_remaining: "List of dimensions not yet reviewed"
          - dimension_coverage: "covered / total dimensions"
          - coverage_age: "Iterations since dimension coverage last changed; require >= 1 before STOP"
          - p0_count: "Count P0 findings across all iterations"
          - p1_count: "Count P1 findings across all iterations"
          - p2_count: "Count P2 findings across all iterations"
          - resource_map_present: "Extract boolean from deep-review-config.json, default false"
          - resource_map_prompt_line: "If resource_map_present is true, return 'Resource Map Coverage: cross-check target_files from {spec_folder}/applied/T-*.md against {spec_folder}/resource-map.md and classify only missed coverage as gaps.' Else return 'Resource Map Coverage: resource-map.md not present; skipping coverage gate.'"
          - session_id: "Extract sessionId from deep-review-config.json"
          - generation: "Extract generation from deep-review-config.json, else 1"
          - last_3_summaries: "Summarize the latest 3 iteration records as 'run N: dimension (ratio, P0/P1/P2)'"
          - latest_status: "Extract status from latest iteration record, else 'none yet'"
          - last_claim_adjudication_passed: "Scan state.jsonl for the most recent `claim_adjudication` event; use its `passed` boolean. Default true when no event exists or when activeP0 + activeP1 == 0 (gate is vacuous without adjudicable findings)."
          - claim_adjudication_active_count: "Count of active P0/P1 findings at the time of the last claim_adjudication event; 0 when none exist"
          - security_sensitive_fix_scope: "Boolean true when the current review is a rerun after fixes involving security, path disclosure, auth/authz, sandboxing, env precedence, public schemas, persistence, or user-visible error payloads; derive from the latest review report, remediation notes, or planning packet when present, else false."
          - fix_completeness_replay_required_rows: "Count prior active or remediated P0/P1 findings, plus any prior security/path P2 findings, that require closed-gate replay before STOP."
          - fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows: "Count required replay rows marked PASS with file:line or command evidence and producer/consumer/matrix coverage."
          - stop_policy: "Extract stopPolicy from deep-review-config.json, default 'convergence'. Valid values: convergence, max-iterations."

      step_check_pause_sentinel:
        action: "Check for pause sentinel file"
        check: "Does {state_paths.pause_sentinel} exist?"
        if_exists:
          set:
            pending_stop_event: "paused"
            pending_stop_reason: "userPaused"
            pending_stop_detail: "sentinel file detected"
          skip_to: step_normalize_pause_events
        if_not_exists:
          proceed: true

      step_graph_convergence:
        action: "Evaluate coverage-graph convergence before the inline review convergence vote"
        inspect:
          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
        transform: |
          1. Call graph convergence on the current review lineage before the inline 3-signal vote.
          2. Record the decision as one of CONTINUE, STOP_ALLOWED, STOP_BLOCKED.
          3. Preserve raw graph signals and blockers for reducer rollup and blocked-stop evidence.
          4. Normalize blockers to both JSON array and CSV forms for downstream stop handling.
          5. Set graph_stop_blocked = true only when graph_decision == "STOP_BLOCKED".
        bash: 'node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/convergence.cjs --spec-folder "{spec_folder}" --loop-type "review" --session-id "{session_id}"'
        note: "Invoke the runtime script directly. Review-side graph convergence must run before the inline review stop vote."
        append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"graph_convergence","mode":"review","run":{current_iteration},"decision":"{graph_decision}","signals":{graph_signals_json},"blockers":{graph_blockers_json},"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{session_id}","generation":{generation}}'
        outputs:
          - graph_decision: "CONTINUE | STOP_ALLOWED | STOP_BLOCKED"
          - graph_signals_json: "JSON object returned by convergence.cjs"
          - graph_blockers_json: "JSON array of graph blockers when present, else []"
          - graph_blockers_csv: "Comma-separated blocker summary when graph blocks STOP, else empty string"
          - graph_stop_blocked: "Boolean true when graph_decision == STOP_BLOCKED"

      step_check_convergence:
        action: "Evaluate graph-assisted review stop conditions and the legal-stop decision tree"
        algorithm: |
          0. CLAIM-ADJUDICATION PRE-CHECK (universal STOP veto; runs before hard stops and composite convergence):
             claim_adjudication_gate_pass = (last_claim_adjudication_passed != false) OR (p0_count + p1_count == 0)
             If claim_adjudication_gate_pass == false:
               candidate_decision = "BLOCKED"
               stop_blocked = true
               stopReason = "blockedStop"
               blocked_gates = ["claimAdjudicationGate"]
               recovery_strategy = "Reviewer must add typed claim-adjudication packets (claim, evidenceRefs, counterevidenceSought, alternativeExplanation, finalSeverity, confidence, downgradeTrigger) for every active P0/P1 finding before STOP is allowed."
               Skip hard stops (1) and composite convergence (4); proceed directly to step 5 legal-stop decision-tree assembly so blocked_by_json, gate outputs, and the blocked_stop event still populate consistently.
          1. HARD STOPS:
             a) if iteration_count >= max_iterations: set decision = "STOP", stopReason = "maxIterationsReached", hard_stop_reason = "max_iterations_reached", preserve any failed legal-stop gates as terminal evidence, and proceed to synthesis without legal-stop veto
             b) if stop_policy == "max-iterations" AND iteration_count < max_iterations: do not allow all-dimensions-clean or composite convergence to stop; treat convergence signals as telemetry, set decision = "CONTINUE", and broaden the next review angle.
             c) if all dimensions clean AND required traceability protocols are covered AND p0_count == 0 AND p1_count == 0 AND coverage_age >= 1 AND all quality gates pass (evidence: every P0/P1 has file:line; scope: all findings within target; coverage: all dimensions reviewed): mark hard_stop_reason = "all_dimensions_clean" and promote to STOP candidate
             Operator stop reasons (`maxIterationsReached`, `manualStop`, `userPaused`, unrecoverable `error`) are terminal ceilings. They do not hide failed gates; they record them in synthesis and stop dispatch.
          2. STUCK DETECTION (threshold 2):
             if stuck_count >= 2: STUCK_RECOVERY
          3. GRAPH CONVERGENCE PREREQUISITE:
               graph_decision, graph_signals_json, and graph_blockers_json are already populated by step_graph_convergence.
               Final STOP is never legal unless the inline review vote says STOP AND graph_decision == "STOP_ALLOWED".
               If graph_decision == "STOP_BLOCKED", set stop_blocked = true and route through blocked_stop emission instead of stopping.
          4. COMPOSITE CONVERGENCE (3-signal weighted vote with split thresholds):
               signals = []
               a) Rolling average (w=0.30, window=2, needs 2+ iterations):
                  avg = mean(last 2 newFindingsRatios)
                  stop if avg <= 0.08
               b) MAD noise floor (w=0.25, needs 3+ iterations):
                  noiseFloor = MAD(all ratios) * 1.4826
                  stop if latest ratio <= noiseFloor
               c) Dimension coverage (w=0.45):
                  coverage = dimensions_covered / total_dimensions
                  stop if coverage >= 1.0 AND required traceability protocols are covered AND coverage_age >= 1
               Redistribute weights for unavailable signals.
               if latest ratio <= 0.05: mark no_progress_threshold_reached
               if stop_policy == "max-iterations" AND iteration_count < max_iterations: convergence votes are telemetry only; set weighted_stop_score for diagnostics, keep decision = "CONTINUE", and choose a broader next focus.
               else if weighted_stop_score >= 0.60: inline STOP becomes a candidate and binary gates must run
          5. LEGAL-STOP DECISION TREE (review-specific gates; runs whenever step 0, all-dimensions-clean, or the composite vote promotes STOP to a candidate; terminal max-iteration/manual/error stops bypass veto and record failed gates as evidence):
               a) convergenceGate: pass when a hard-stop condition is met OR weighted_stop_score >= 0.60; record score
               b) dimensionCoverageGate: pass when configured dimensions and required traceability coverage are complete and coverage_age >= 1; record covered and missing dimensions
               c) p0ResolutionGate: pass when activeP0 == 0; record activeP0
               d) evidenceDensityGate: pass when every active P0/P1 has concrete file:line evidence and no active P0/P1 relies only on inference; record avgEvidencePerFinding
               e) hotspotSaturationGate: pass when review hotspots have been revisited enough times to satisfy the saturation heuristic
               f) claimAdjudicationGate: pass when claim_adjudication_gate_pass == true (i.e. last_claim_adjudication_passed != false OR there are no active P0/P1 findings to adjudicate); record last_claim_adjudication_passed and claim_adjudication_active_count
               g) fixCompletenessReplayGate: pass when security_sensitive_fix_scope == false OR every required closed-gate replay row passes with file:line or command evidence and producer/consumer/matrix coverage; record security_sensitive_fix_scope, fix_completeness_replay_required_rows, and fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows
               h) candidateCoverageGate: skip entirely when `reviewDepthSchemaVersion` is not 2 on the latest iteration. Otherwise pass when the current iteration's `reviewDepthApplicability.scopeClass` is `trivial` with `enforcement: "skip"` AND `reviewDepthApplicability.evidenceRefs` is non-empty, OR when the reducer registry's `searchDebt` is empty AND `candidateCoverage.covered` is a superset of `searchCoverage.requiredBugClasses` for the lineage. Otherwise pass = false; record `searchDebt` items as blocked-stop evidence; blocker name = "candidateCoverageGate"; recovery_strategy = "Resolve deferred/blocked obligations in next iteration or downgrade scope explicitly to trivial with cited evidence."
               i) graphlessFallbackGate: skip when `reviewDepthSchemaVersion` is not 2 OR the reducer registry's last-iteration `searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode` is `graph`. Applies when graphCoverageMode is `graphless_fallback` or `unavailable_blocked`. Pass when graphCoverageMode is `graphless_fallback` AND at least one `searchLedger` row exists for each entry in `searchCoverage.requiredBugClasses` with `searchActions[].method` in {"direct_read","exact_grep","semantic_search","code_graph_status_check","producer_consumer_trace","negative_test_inspection"} AND non-empty `evidenceRefs`. Fail automatically when graphCoverageMode is `unavailable_blocked`; record the explicit unavailability reason. Otherwise pass = false; blocker name = "graphlessFallbackGate"; recovery_strategy = "Add cited fallback ledger rows (direct read, exact search, producer/consumer trace, negative-test inspection) or set graphCoverageMode to 'graph' once graph data is available."
             if STOP is a candidate and any gate fails:
               decision = "BLOCKED"
               stop_blocked = true
               stopReason = "blockedStop"
               blocked_by_json = JSON array of failing gate names (always include "claimAdjudicationGate" when claim_adjudication_gate_pass == false, "fixCompletenessReplayGate" when fix_completeness_replay_gate_pass == false, "candidateCoverageGate" when candidate_coverage_gate_pass == false, and "graphlessFallbackGate" when graphless_fallback_gate_pass == false)
               blocked_by_csv = comma-separated list of failing gate names
               convergence_gate_pass = boolean
               convergence_gate_score = numeric convergence score
               dimension_coverage_gate_pass = boolean
               dimension_coverage_gate_covered_json = JSON array of covered review dimensions
               dimension_coverage_gate_missing_json = JSON array of missing review dimensions
               p0_resolution_gate_pass = boolean
               active_p0_count = number of active P0 findings
               evidence_density_gate_pass = boolean
               avg_evidence_per_finding = numeric evidence density
               hotspot_saturation_gate_pass = boolean
               claim_adjudication_gate_pass = boolean
               claim_adjudication_active_count = numeric count of active P0/P1 at the gate
               fix_completeness_replay_gate_pass = boolean
               security_sensitive_fix_scope = boolean
               fix_completeness_replay_required_rows = numeric count of closed-gate replay rows required for security-sensitive fix reruns
               fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows = numeric count of required closed-gate replay rows marked PASS with evidence
               candidate_coverage_gate_pass = boolean
               candidate_coverage_search_debt_json = JSON array of reducer-owned searchDebt obligations blocking STOP, else []
               candidate_coverage_missing_json = JSON array of required bug classes not present in candidateCoverage.covered, else []
               graphless_fallback_gate_pass = boolean
               graphless_fallback_mode = latest reducer-owned searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode value
               graphless_fallback_missing_json = JSON array of required bug classes without cited fallback ledger rows, else []
               graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason = explicit reason when graphCoverageMode == "unavailable_blocked", else empty string
               recovery_strategy = one-line hint describing the next review action needed to satisfy the failed gates; combine candidateCoverageGate and graphlessFallbackGate recovery guidance when both fail (when claimAdjudicationGate is the failing gate, prefer the step-0 recovery_strategy already set)
          6. COMBINED STOP RULE:
               a) if STOP is legal AND graph_decision == "STOP_ALLOWED": decision = "STOP"
               b) if STOP is legal AND graph_decision == "STOP_BLOCKED":
                    decision = "BLOCKED"
                    stop_blocked = true
                    stopReason = "blockedStop"
                    # Blocked-stop closure: blockedBy must be string[] per the
                    # published review blocked-stop contract. Extract the `.type`
                    # field from each structured graph blocker; preserve the full
                    # objects in graph_blocker_detail_json for operator inspection.
                    blocked_by_json = JSON.stringify(graph_blockers_json.map(b => typeof b === "string" ? b : (b && b.type) || "graph_blocker"))
                    blocked_by_csv = graph_blockers_csv
                    graph_blocker_detail_json = graph_blockers_json
                    recovery_strategy = "Resolve review coverage-graph blockers before stopping."
               c) if STOP is legal AND graph_decision == "CONTINUE":
                    decision = "CONTINUE"
                    stop_blocked = false
                    reason = "graph_convergence_continue"
               d) else keep the existing STUCK_RECOVERY or CONTINUE outcome
        outputs:
          - decision: "STOP | BLOCKED | STUCK_RECOVERY | CONTINUE"
          - reason: "Human-readable stop reason"
          - stop_blocked: "Boolean true when STOP is vetoed by inline legal-stop gates or graph_decision == STOP_BLOCKED"
          - blocked_by_json: "JSON array of failed legal-stop gates when decision == BLOCKED"
          - blocked_by_csv: "Comma-separated list of failed legal-stop gates when decision == BLOCKED"
          - convergence_gate_pass: "Boolean pass/fail for convergenceGate"
          - convergence_gate_score: "Numeric score recorded for convergenceGate"
          - dimension_coverage_gate_pass: "Boolean pass/fail for dimensionCoverageGate"
          - dimension_coverage_gate_covered_json: "JSON array of covered review dimensions"
          - dimension_coverage_gate_missing_json: "JSON array of missing review dimensions"
          - p0_resolution_gate_pass: "Boolean pass/fail for p0ResolutionGate"
          - active_p0_count: "Active P0 finding count at stop-check time"
          - evidence_density_gate_pass: "Boolean pass/fail for evidenceDensityGate"
          - avg_evidence_per_finding: "Numeric evidence density for active findings"
          - hotspot_saturation_gate_pass: "Boolean pass/fail for hotspotSaturationGate"
          - claim_adjudication_gate_pass: "Boolean pass/fail for claimAdjudicationGate (false vetoes STOP)"
          - claim_adjudication_active_count: "Count of active P0/P1 findings evaluated by claimAdjudicationGate"
          - fix_completeness_replay_gate_pass: "Boolean pass/fail for fixCompletenessReplayGate (false vetoes STOP on security-sensitive fix reruns)"
          - security_sensitive_fix_scope: "Boolean true when the review is a security-sensitive fix rerun"
          - fix_completeness_replay_required_rows: "Count of closed-gate replay rows required before STOP"
          - fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows: "Count of required closed-gate replay rows that passed with evidence"
          - candidate_coverage_gate_pass: "Boolean pass/fail for candidateCoverageGate (false vetoes STOP for unresolved reducer-owned search debt or uncovered required bug classes)"
          - candidate_coverage_search_debt_json: "JSON array of reducer-owned searchDebt obligations used as blocked-stop evidence"
          - candidate_coverage_missing_json: "JSON array of required bug classes missing from candidateCoverage.covered"
          - graphless_fallback_gate_pass: "Boolean pass/fail for graphlessFallbackGate (false vetoes STOP when graphless fallback evidence is absent or unavailable_blocked)"
          - graphless_fallback_mode: "Latest reducer-owned searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode value"
          - graphless_fallback_missing_json: "JSON array of required bug classes missing cited fallback ledger rows"
          - graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason: "Explicit unavailability reason when graphCoverageMode == unavailable_blocked"
          - graph_decision: "CONTINUE | STOP_ALLOWED | STOP_BLOCKED from step_graph_convergence"
          - graph_signals_json: "JSON object returned by graph convergence"
          - graph_blockers_json: "JSON array of graph blockers"
          - graph_blockers_csv: "Comma-separated graph blocker summary"
          - recovery_strategy: "One-line hint describing the next review action when decision == BLOCKED or STUCK_RECOVERY"

      step_emit_blocked_stop:
        action: "Persist blocked-stop evidence whenever the review legal-stop decision tree vetoes STOP"
        if_blocked:
          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"blocked_stop","mode":"review","run":{current_iteration},"blockedBy":{blocked_by_json},"gateResults":{"convergenceGate":{"pass":{convergence_gate_pass},"score":{convergence_gate_score}},"dimensionCoverageGate":{"pass":{dimension_coverage_gate_pass},"covered":{dimension_coverage_gate_covered_json},"missing":{dimension_coverage_gate_missing_json}},"p0ResolutionGate":{"pass":{p0_resolution_gate_pass},"activeP0":{active_p0_count}},"evidenceDensityGate":{"pass":{evidence_density_gate_pass},"avgEvidencePerFinding":{avg_evidence_per_finding}},"hotspotSaturationGate":{"pass":{hotspot_saturation_gate_pass}},"claimAdjudicationGate":{"pass":{claim_adjudication_gate_pass},"activeP0P1":{claim_adjudication_active_count}},"fixCompletenessReplayGate":{"pass":{fix_completeness_replay_gate_pass},"securitySensitive":{security_sensitive_fix_scope},"requiredRows":{fix_completeness_replay_required_rows},"passingRows":{fix_completeness_replay_passing_rows}},"candidateCoverageGate":{"pass":{candidate_coverage_gate_pass},"searchDebt":{candidate_coverage_search_debt_json},"missing":{candidate_coverage_missing_json}},"graphlessFallbackGate":{"pass":{graphless_fallback_gate_pass},"mode":"{graphless_fallback_mode}","missing":{graphless_fallback_missing_json},"unavailabilityReason":"{graphless_fallback_unavailability_reason}"}},"graphBlockerDetail":{graph_blockers_json},"recoveryStrategy":"{recovery_strategy}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{session_id}","generation":{generation}}'
          log: "Blocked STOP persisted for review loop. Failed gates: {blocked_by_csv}. Recovery strategy: {recovery_strategy}"
        if_not_blocked:
          proceed: true

      step_handle_convergence:
        action: "Act on convergence decision"
        if_stop:
          log: "Loop stopping. Reason: {reason}"
          skip_to: phase_synthesis
        if_blocked:
          log: "Legal-stop gates blocked STOP. Failed gates: {blocked_by_csv}. Continuing review."
        if_stuck_recovery:
          log: "Stuck detected ({stuck_count} consecutive no-progress). Entering recovery."
          recovery_strategies:
            - "Change granularity: file-level → function-level → line-level review"
            - "Traceability protocol replay: re-run unresolved core or overlay checks"
            - "Escalate severity review: re-examine P2 findings for potential P1 upgrades"
          pivot_target: "Pivot to the least-covered dimension (the review dimension with the fewest iteration passes and lowest coverage ratio)."
          set:
            next_dimension: "RECOVERY: {recovery_strategy}. Least-covered dimension: {least_explored}"
            pending_stop_event: "stuck_recovery"
            pending_stop_reason: "stuckRecovery"
            pending_stop_detail: "Recovery path activated after repeated no-progress iterations"
          reset: { stuck_count: 0 }
        if_continue:
          log: "Iteration {current_iteration}: Continuing. Dimension: {next_dimension}"

      step_normalize_pause_events:
        action: "Rewrite raw pause and recovery events to the frozen STOP_REASONS enum at emission time: converged, maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, stuckRecovery, error, manualStop, userPaused"
        if_pending_pause_event:
          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"userPaused","mode":"review","stopReason":"userPaused","reason":"{pending_stop_detail}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{session_id}","generation":{generation}}'
          message: "Review paused. Delete review/.deep-review-pause to resume."
          reset:
            pending_stop_event: null
            pending_stop_reason: null
            pending_stop_detail: null
          halt: true
        if_pending_stuck_recovery:
          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"stuckRecovery","mode":"review","stopReason":"stuckRecovery","fromIteration":{current_iteration},"strategy":"{recovery_strategy}","targetDimension":"{least_explored}","outcome":"pending","reason":"{pending_stop_detail}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{session_id}","generation":{generation}}'
          reset:
            pending_stop_event: null
            pending_stop_reason: null
            pending_stop_detail: null
        if_no_pending_event:
          proceed: true

      step_generate_state_summary:
        action: "Generate compact state summary for dispatch context (~200 tokens)"
        generate: |
          STATE SUMMARY (auto-generated):
          Iteration: {current_iteration} of {max_iterations}
          Dimension: {next_dimension}
          Prior Findings: P0={p0_count} P1={p1_count} P2={p2_count}
          Dimension Coverage: {dimensions_covered_list} ({dimension_coverage})
          Traceability: core={core_protocol_status} overlay={overlay_protocol_status}
          {resource_map_prompt_line}
          Coverage Age: {coverage_age}
          Last 2 ratios: {ratio_prev} -> {ratio_latest}
          Stuck count: {stuck_count}
          Provisional Verdict: {PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL|PENDING} hasAdvisories={has_advisories}

      step_marker_scan:
        action: "H-2 loop-prevention guard: scan previous iteration's rendered prompt for nested review marker"
        algorithm: |
          if current_iteration > 1:
            PREV_PROMPT_PATH = "{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{previous_iteration}.md"
            if file_exists(PREV_PROMPT_PATH):
              PREV_PROMPT_FIRST_LINE = head -1 of PREV_PROMPT_PATH
              if PREV_PROMPT_FIRST_LINE matches ^(DEEP-REVIEW|DEEP-RESEARCH|CODE-REVIEW)$:
                log "ERROR: nested review-of-review loop detected (marker found in previous iteration prompt). Aborting."
                halt with exit code 2
        note: |
          False-positive prevention: only matches when the first line is exactly one of the
          three canonical markers. Markers appearing in comments or body text are ignored.
          True-positive triggers only when a previous iteration's rendered prompt literally
          starts with a deep-loop marker, indicating a nested review-of-review dispatch.

      step_dispatch_iteration:
        action: "Dispatch @deep-review agent for one review iteration"
        resolve_executor:
          source: "config.executor"
          loader: ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts#parseExecutorConfig"
        pre_dispatch:
          generate_summary: |
            STATE SUMMARY (auto-generated):
            Iteration: {current_iteration} of {max_iterations}
            Dimension: {next_dimension}
            Prior Findings: P0={p0_count} P1={p1_count} P2={p2_count}
            Dimension Coverage: {dimensions_covered_list} ({dimension_coverage})
            Traceability: core={core_protocol_status} overlay={overlay_protocol_status}
            {resource_map_prompt_line}
            Coverage Age: {coverage_age}
            Last 2 ratios: {ratio_prev} -> {ratio_latest}
            Stuck count: {stuck_count}
            Provisional Verdict: {PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL|PENDING} hasAdvisories={has_advisories}
          render_prompt_pack:
            template_path: ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl"
            renderer: ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/prompt-pack.ts#renderPromptPack"
            output_path: "{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{current_iteration}.md"
            variables:
              state_summary: "{state_summary}"
              current_iteration: "{current_iteration}"
              max_iterations: "{max_iterations}"
              next_dimension: "{next_dimension}"
              review_target: "{review_target}"
              review_scope_files: "{review_scope_files}"
              p0_count: "{p0_count}"
              p1_count: "{p1_count}"
              p2_count: "{p2_count}"
              state_paths_config: "{state_paths.config}"
              state_paths_state_log: "{state_paths.state_log}"
              state_paths_findings_registry: "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
              state_paths_strategy: "{state_paths.strategy}"
              state_paths_iteration_pattern: "{state_paths.iteration_pattern}"
              state_paths_delta_pattern: "{state_paths.delta_pattern}"
        pre_dispatch_audit:
          skip_when: "config.executor.kind == 'native'"
          call: ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts#writeFirstRecordExecutor"
          args:
            stateLogPath: "{state_paths.state_log}"
            executor: "{config.executor}"
            iteration: "{current_iteration}"
        branch_on: "config.executor.kind"
        if_native:
          dispatch:
            agent: deep-review
            model: opus
            context_source: "rendered_prompt_pack"
          wait_for_completion: true
        if_cli_copilot:
          command: |
            node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module <<'EOF'
            import { readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
            import { spawnSync } from 'node:child_process';

            import { buildCopilotPromptArg } from './.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts';

            const promptPath = '{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{current_iteration}.md';
            const prompt = readFileSync(promptPath, 'utf8');

            // Workflow-resolved spec folder is the only legal write authority.
            // The helper (`validateSpecFolder` inside `buildCopilotPromptArg`)
            // is the single source of truth for rejecting empty / unresolved
            // template / whitespace-only / string-coerced empty values. The
            // YAML hands the raw `{spec_folder}` directly; on rejection the
            // helper safe-fails to `type:"missing", writeIntent:true` (Gate-3
            // enforcement, --allow-all-tools stripped).
            const specFolderRaw = '{spec_folder}';
            const targetAuthority = { type: 'approved', specFolder: specFolderRaw };

            const baseArgs = [
              '-p',
              prompt,
              '--model',
              '{config.executor.model}',
              '--allow-all-tools',
              '--no-ask-user',
            ];

            const built = buildCopilotPromptArg({
              promptPath,","numLines":848,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1604,"truncatedByTokenCap":true}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01AsmZNrGbN9GqzdypAfgBi6","type":"tool_result","content":"849	              prompt,
850	              targetAuthority,
851	              baseArgv: baseArgs,
852	            });
853	
854	            // Large-prompt @PROMPT_PATH wrapper: helper emits a `promptFileBody`
855	            // containing `<TARGET AUTHORITY preamble>\n\n---\n\n<original>`.
856	            // Write it to disk so the file Copilot reads via @path opens with
857	            // the authority block. Without this, recovered/bootstrap folder

1043	        transform: |

1045	          2. If graphEvents is absent or empty, set graph_nodes_json = [] and graph_edges_json = [] and skip the upsert.
1046	          3. For each graph event without relation/source/target, normalize type = uppercase(event.type || event.nodeKind || event.type) and keep only DIMENSION, FILE, FINDING, EVIDENCE, REMEDIATION, BUG_CLASS, INVARIANT, PRODUCER, CONSUMER, TEST.
1047	          4. Emit nodes as { id, type, name, iteration, metadata } using the event label (or id fallback) and review metadata for run/current focus.
1048	          5. For each graph event with relation + source + target, normalize relation = uppercase(event.relation) and keep only COVERS, EVIDENCE_FOR, CONTRADICTS, RESOLVES, CONFIRMS, ESCALATES, IN_DIMENSION, IN_FILE.
1049	          6. Emit edges as { id, relation, sourceId, targetId, metadata } and deduplicate the batch by id.
1050	          7. Discard malformed or unknown graph events instead of failing the review iteration.

1130	  phase_synthesis:
1131	    purpose: Compile all iteration findings into review/review-report.md
1132	    steps:
1133	
1134	      step_fanout_merge:

1153	          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
1154	          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
1155	        extract:
1156	          - iteration_count: "Count lines where type === 'iteration'"
1157	          - p0_count: "Total P0 findings across all iterations (deduped)"
1158	          - p1_count: "Total P1 findings across all iterations (deduped)"
1159	          - p2_count: "Total P2 findings across all iterations (deduped)"
1160	          - dimensions_covered: "List of reviewed dimensions"
1161	          - dimension_coverage: "covered / total"
1162	          - ac_coverage_signal: "For spec-folder targets only: if level is 2+ and checklist.md exists and implementation-summary.md is in-progress or later, surface the AC_COVERAGE advisory status; otherwise mark exempt."
1163	          - last_3_summaries: "Summarize the latest 3 iteration records"
1164	          - reason: "Use the latest stop reason if present, else the frozen default 'converged'. (Must be a member of the STOP_REASONS enum: converged, maxIterationsReached, blockedStop, stuckRecovery, error, manualStop, userPaused.)"
1165	
1166	      # H-1 verdict derivation: parse findings JSONL, map highest severity to verdict
1167	      # - PASS: no P0 or P1 findings (P2-only or clean)
1168	      # - CONDITIONAL: any P1 findings (no P0)
1169	      # - FAIL: any P0 findings
1170	      # Final line of iteration-NNN.md MUST be exactly: "Review verdict: PASS" / "Review verdict: CONDITIONAL" / "Review verdict: FAIL"
1171	      step_derive_verdict:
1172	        action: "Derive review verdict from accumulated findings"
1173	        algorithm: |
1174	          if p0_count > 0:
1175	            verdict = "FAIL"
1176	          elif p1_count > 0:
1177	            verdict = "CONDITIONAL"
1178	          else:
1179	            verdict = "PASS"
1180	          has_advisories = (verdict == "PASS" and p2_count > 0)
1181	          ac_coverage_signal is advisory while the validation rule is INFO/default-off; include it in synthesis traceability, but do not change the exact verdict enum from this signal alone.
1182	        outputs:
1183	          - verdict: "PASS | CONDITIONAL | FAIL"
1184	          - has_advisories: "Boolean true when verdict is PASS and any active P2 findings exist"
1185	          - ac_coverage_signal: "exempt | disabled | pass | advisory-shortfall"
1186	        contract: |
1187	          Every iteration-NNN.md must end with exactly: "Review verdict: PASS" (or CONDITIONAL or FAIL).
1188	          Downstream automation parses this final line via exact string match — do not vary the format.

1195	      step_build_finding_registry:
1196	        action: "Build deduplicated finding registry from all iterations"
1197	        algorithm: |
1198	          1. Extract all findings from iteration JSONL delta files
1199	          2. Compute content_hash for each finding (emission requirement):
1200	             content_hash = sha256(file_path + "\u001f" + line_range + "\u001f" + finding_type + "\u001f" + normalized_description_80chars)
1201	             Normalized description = whitespace-collapsed, lowercased, first 80 chars of the finding description.
1202	          3. Two-tier dedup:
1203	             PRIMARY: group by content_hash field
1204	               - Same content_hash, multiple dimensions → single finding, merged `dimensions: [<all>]` array
1205	               - Keep highest severity on duplicates
1206	             FALLBACK (legacy records lacking content_hash): file:line + normalized_title
1207	               - Same file:line, multiple dimensions → single finding, multiple dimension tags
1208	               - Keep highest severity on duplicates
1209	          4. Track finding lifecycle:
1210	             - status: active | duplicate | upgraded | downgraded | contested | resolved_false_positive
1211	             - Record first_seen, upgraded_in, previous_severity for severity changes
1212	             - Record dimensions array reflecting all dimensions that emitted this finding
1213	          5. Resolve contradictions:
1214	             - Findings marked contested across iterations → referee pass
1215	             - Determine final severity and status
1216	          6. Count only 'active' findings for totals
1217	        outputs:
1218	          - finding_registry: "Deduplicated list of all findings with metadata"
1219	          - active_p0: "Count of active P0 findings"
1220	          - active_p1: "Count of active P1 findings"
1221	          - active_p2: "Count of active P2 findings"
1222	
1223	      step_adversarial_selfcheck:
1224	        action: "Final adversarial self-check on all P0 and P1 findings"
1225	        algorithm: |
1226	          For each active P0/P1 finding:
1227	            1. Hunter: Re-verify finding against source code (re-read file:line)
1228	            2. Skeptic: Challenge severity — is this really P0/P1? Could it be P2?
1229	            3. Referee: Final call — confirm, downgrade, or mark false_positive
1230	          Update finding_registry with adjudicated results.

1302	      step_convergence_report:
1303	        action: "Append convergence report to JSONL"
1304	        command: |
1305	          node --input-type=module <<'EOF'
1306	          import { appendFileSync, existsSync, readFileSync, statSync } from 'node:fs';
1307	
1308	          const paths = {
1309	            stateLog: String.raw`{state_paths.state_log}`,
1310	            registry: String.raw`{state_paths.findings_registry}`,
1311	            review: String.raw`{state_paths.review_output}`,
1312	            dashboard: String.raw`{state_paths.dashboard}`,
1313	          };
1314	
1315	          function hasFile(filePath) {
1316	            try {
1317	              return existsSync(filePath) && statSync(filePath).isFile();
1318	            } catch {
1319	              return false;
1320	            }
1321	          }
1322	
1323	          function readJson(filePath) {
1324	            try {
1325	              return JSON.parse(readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'));
1326	            } catch {
1327	              return null;
1328	            }
1329	          }
1330	
1331	          function readJsonl(filePath) {
1332	            if (!hasFile(filePath)) return [];
1333	            return readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8')
1334	              .split(/\r?\n/)
1335	              .map((line) => line.trim())
1336	              .filter(Boolean)
1337	              .flatMap((line) => {
1338	                try {
1339	                  return [JSON.parse(line)];
1340	                } catch {
1341	                  return [];
1342	                }
1343	              });
1344	          }
1345	
1346	          function countIterationFindings(record) {
1347	            if (!record || record.type !== 'iteration') return 0;
1348	            const numeric = Number(record.findingsCount);
1349	            if (Number.isFinite(numeric) && numeric > 0) return Math.floor(numeric);
1350	            if (Array.isArray(record.findingDetails)) return record.findingDetails.length;
1351	            return 0;
1352	          }
1353	
1354	          function normalizeFindingKey(value) {
1355	            return String(value || '').trim().toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, ' ');
1356	          }
1357	
1358	          function findingKeys(candidate) {
1359	            if (typeof candidate === 'string') {
1360	              const key = normalizeFindingKey(candidate);
1361	              return key ? [key] : [];
1362	            }
1363	            if (!candidate || typeof candidate !== 'object' || Array.isArray(candidate)) return [];
1364	            return [...new Set([
1365	              candidate.id,
1366	              candidate.findingId,
1367	              candidate.title,
1368	              candidate.summary,
1369	              candidate.text,
1370	              candidate.finding,
1371	              candidate.description,
1372	            ].map(normalizeFindingKey).filter(Boolean))];
1373	          }
1374	
1375	          function collectIterationFindingGroups(record) {
1376	            if (!record || record.type !== 'iteration' || !Array.isArray(record.findingDetails)) return [];
1377	            return record.findingDetails
1378	              .map(findingKeys)
1379	              .filter((keys) => keys.length > 0);
1380	          }
1381	
1382	          function collectRegistryFindingKeys(registry) {
1383	            return [registry?.openFindings, registry?.resolvedFindings, registry?.repeatedFindings]
1384	              .filter(Array.isArray)
1385	              .flatMap((findings) => findings)
1386	              .flatMap(findingKeys);
1387	          }
1388	
1389	          function appendEvent(event) {
1390	            appendFileSync(paths.stateLog, `${JSON.stringify(event)}\n`, 'utf8');
1391	          }
1392	
1393	          const registry = readJson(paths.registry);
1394	          const openCount = Array.isArray(registry?.openFindings) ? registry.openFindings.length : null;
1395	          const resolvedCount = Array.isArray(registry?.resolvedFindings) ? registry.resolvedFindings.length : 0;
1396	          const repeatedCount = Array.isArray(registry?.repeatedFindings) ? registry.repeatedFindings.length : 0;
1397	          const registryFindingCount = openCount === null ? null : openCount + resolvedCount + repeatedCount;
1398	          const stateRecords = readJsonl(paths.stateLog);
1399	          const iterationFindingCount = stateRecords.reduce((sum, record) => sum + countIterationFindings(record), 0);
1400	          const identifiableFindingGroups = stateRecords.flatMap(collectIterationFindingGroups);
1401	          const registryFindingKeys = new Set(collectRegistryFindingKeys(registry));
1402	          const missingStructuredFindingCount = identifiableFindingGroups.filter((keys) => !keys.some((key) => registryFindingKeys.has(key))).length;
1403	          const identifiableFindingCount = identifiableFindingGroups.length;
1404	          const missingArtifacts = [
1405	            ['registry', paths.registry],
1406	            ['review', paths.review],
1407	            ['dashboard', paths.dashboard],
1408	          ].filter(([, filePath]) => !hasFile(filePath)).map(([name, filePath]) => ({ name, path: filePath }));
1409	          const invariantFailures = [];
1410	          if (missingArtifacts.length > 0) invariantFailures.push('missing_synthesis_artifacts');
1411	          if (registryFindingCount === null) invariantFailures.push('registry_missing_openFindings');
1412	          if (iterationFindingCount > 0 && (registryFindingCount ?? 0) === 0) invariantFailures.push('state_findings_not_reflected_in_registry');
1413	          if (identifiableFindingCount > 0 && registryFindingCount !== null && registryFindingCount < identifiableFindingCount) invariantFailures.push('structured_state_findings_partially_reflected_in_registry');
1414	          if (missingStructuredFindingCount > 0) invariantFailures.push('structured_state_findings_missing_from_registry');
1415	
1416	          if (invariantFailures.length > 0) {
1417	            appendEvent({
1418	              type: 'event',
1419	              event: 'synthesis_incomplete',
1420	              mode: 'review',
1421	              severity: 'warning',
1422	              totalIterations: Number('{iteration_count}'),
1423	              activeP0: Number('{active_p0}'),
1424	              activeP1: Number('{active_p1}'),
1425	              activeP2: Number('{active_p2}'),
1426	              dimensionCoverage: Number('{dimension_coverage}'),
1427	              verdict: '{verdict}',
1428	              releaseReadinessState: '{release_readiness_state}',
1429	              stopReason: '{reason}',
1430	              reason: 'synthesis_artifact_invariant_failed',
1431	              invariantFailures,
1432	              missingArtifacts,
1433	              registryFindingCount,
1434	              iterationFindingCount,
1435	              identifiableFindingCount,
1436	              missingStructuredFindingCount,
1437	              timestamp: '{ISO_8601_NOW}',
1438	            });
1439	          } else {
1440	            appendEvent({
1441	              type: 'event',
1442	              event: 'synthesis_complete',
1443	              mode: 'review',
1444	              totalIterations: Number('{iteration_count}'),
1445	              activeP0: Number('{active_p0}'),
1446	              activeP1: Number('{active_p1}'),
1447	              activeP2: Number('{active_p2}'),
1448	              dimensionCoverage: Number('{dimension_coverage}'),
1449	              verdict: '{verdict}',
1450	              releaseReadinessState: '{release_readiness_state}',
1451	              stopReason: '{reason}',
1452	              timestamp: '{ISO_8601_NOW}',
1453	            });
1454	          }
1455	          EOF

            - "Large-prompt path relies on copilot honoring @path references within its prompt string"

            - "If spec_folder is missing/malformed/unresolved on a write-intent dispatch, the helper enforces plan-only by replacing the prompt with a Gate-3 question and stripping --allow-all-tools"
